MeGA is a hybrid neural rendering system that combines mesh-based and Gaussian splatting techniques to create photorealistic head avatars. The system uses FLAME mesh representation for facial geometry and Gaussian splatting for hair representation, enabling both high-quality rendering and flexible editing operations.

The system operates on the NeRSemble dataset and supports training personalized avatars for multiple subjects with capabilities for hair transfer between subjects and texture editing through 2D painting optimization.

MeGA employs a hybrid architecture that separates face and hair representation to leverage the strengths of both mesh-based and point-based rendering approaches.

The Gaussian Surfels project is a system for high-quality 3D surface reconstruction using Gaussian primitives. It leverages 3D Gaussian Splatting technology to create detailed, accurate surface models from multi-view images. This wiki page provides a high-level introduction to the system's architecture, key components, and workflow.

- High-quality 3D surface reconstruction from multi-view images - Differentiable rendering for optimization-based reconstruction - Surface normal prediction and integration - Mesh extraction via Poisson surface reconstruction - Geometric accuracy evaluation via Chamfer distance

The system works with structured datasets (DTU and BlendedMVS) and can be adapted to work with custom image sets when processed with COLMAP for structure-from-motion.

WaEnhancer (Wa Enhancer) is an Xposed Framework module that enhances WhatsApp and WhatsApp Business by adding privacy controls, UI customization options, media improvements, and general quality-of-life features. It operates by dynamically discovering and hooking obfuscated WhatsApp code at runtime using DexKit, allowing it to modify application behavior without requiring source code modifications or reverse engineering for each WhatsApp update.

WaEnhancer operates exclusively within WhatsApp's process space using the Xposed Framework. It provides over 40 distinct features organized into four main categories, supporting both standard WhatsApp and WhatsApp Business through dual build flavors.

Bootstrap-2D-GS is a 3D Gaussian Splatting system that enhances the original 3D Gaussian Splatting method with latent diffusion models (LDM) for improved novel view synthesis and scene bootstrapping. This system extends the real-time radiance field rendering capabilities with generative AI to create higher quality 3D scene representations from limited input views.

Bootstrap-2D-GS consists of five primary subsystems that work together to create enhanced 3D Gaussian representations:

The main training pipeline is orchestrated by `train.py`, which manages the optimization of 3D Gaussian parameters through differentiable rendering:

Updater-KMP is a multi-platform application designed to retrieve and display Xiaomi official recovery ROM information. Built with Kotlin Multiplatform and Compose Multiplatform, it offers a consistent user experience across multiple platforms while using platform-specific optimizations where necessary. This document provides a high-level introduction to the project's purpose, architecture, and main features.
EasyLauncher - Minimal and Clutter Free Android launcher

The FUTO Android Keyboard is an open-source customizable keyboard application for Android devices. It builds upon the foundation of the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) Latin IME (Input Method Editor) while adding modern features and a redesigned user interface. This document provides a high-level overview of the keyboard's architecture, components, and how they interact with each other.

This keyboard application aims to provide a privacy-focused, customizable, and feature-rich typing experience. It includes support for multiple languages, predictive text through various dictionary systems, themes, keyboard layout options, and special functions like emoji picking and clipboard management.
Official Repo for Fine-Tuning Large Vision-Language Models as Decision-Making Agents via Reinforcement Learning

Open-LLaVA-NeXT is an open-source implementation designed to reproduce the LLaVA-NeXT series of large multimodal models. This repository provides a complete training and evaluation framework for building vision-language models that can understand and reason about images through natural language interactions.

The system implements a two-stage training pipeline that aligns visual and textual representations, then fine-tunes the entire model for visual instruction following. For detailed information about the training infrastructure, see Training System. For evaluation methodologies and benchmarks, see Evaluation System. For available pretrained models and performance metrics, see Model Zoo and Documentation.

mpvKt is an Android media player application built on the libmpv engine, designed with a modern Jetpack Compose UI and comprehensive customization capabilities. The application provides local file playback, URL streaming support, and extensive user configuration through a multi-layered preferences system.

mpvKt serves as a feature-rich video player with focus on: - Native integration with libmpv via `MPVLib` for high-performance playback - Reactive state management using Kotlin `StateFlow` for UI updates - Persistent playback state restoration via Room database - Extensible customization through Lua scripting and custom buttons - Android system integration (PiP, media sessions, background playback)

RLAIF-V (Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback - Vision) is an open-source framework designed to align Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) with the goal of achieving super GPT-4V trustworthiness. The system leverages AI feedback for training and focuses on reducing hallucination in vision-language tasks. This page provides a high-level overview of the RLAIF-V architecture, components, and workflow.

RLAIF-V maximizes the potential of open-source feedback from two key perspectives:

1. Generating high-quality feedback data that effectively reduces hallucinations in MLLMs 2. Implementing online feedback learning algorithms for iterative model improvement

The Sports repository was created to push the boundaries of computer vision in sports analytics, where precision, timing, and spatial awareness are critical. It addresses several key challenges in sports video analysis:

- Ball tracking in high-speed situations - Player detection, tracking, and team classification - Reading jersey numbers - Field/pitch detection and analysis - Camera calibration for accurate spatial measurements - Player re-identification when they leave and re-enter the frame

The repository provides modular, reusable components that can be applied across different sports and similar computer vision domains.

The repository follows a modular architecture with several interconnected systems:

OuterTune is a feature-rich Android music player application that uniquely combines local music playback with YouTube Music streaming capabilities. This document provides a high-level overview of the system architecture, core components, and the relationships between them. It serves as an entry point to understand how OuterTune is structured and how its various parts work together.

OuterTune is built as a fork of InnerTune with significant enhancements, offering:

EasyNotes is a lightweight, privacy-focused Android notes application built around Material You design principles. The app aims to provide a clean, efficient note-taking experience with rich formatting options while maintaining strict privacy standards and requiring zero permissions.

Key philosophies guiding the design: - Minimalism with powerful features - Strong privacy and security by default - Full Markdown support without complexity - Native Android integration with Material You

The EasyNotes application follows a clean architecture approach with clear separation of concerns between data, domain, and presentation layers.

- app/src/main/java/com/kin/easynotes/presentation/MainActivity.kt - app/src/main/

Table-LLaVA is a multimodal Large Language and Vision Assistant specialized for understanding tables directly from images. Unlike conventional methods that require tables to be converted to textual formats (like Markdown or HTML), Table-LLaVA processes table images directly to generate responses to various table-related queries. This capability is particularly valuable in real-world scenarios like scanned documents and webpage screenshots where high-quality textual table representations are difficult to extract.

The system builds upon the LLaVA v1.5 architecture with specific adaptations for table understanding. Table-LLaVA consists of two primary components:

DOGS extends traditional 3D Gaussian Splatting with distributed training capabilities, supporting both single-GPU and multi-GPU ADMM consensus training. The system processes large-scale datasets from multiple sources (Mill-19, UrbanScene3D, MatrixCity, custom videos) and provides comprehensive training, evaluation, and visualization tools.

MVSGaussian is a fast, generalizable 3D Gaussian representation system for efficient scene reconstruction from multi-view images. This page provides a comprehensive overview of the MVSGaussian system architecture, its key components, and primary workflows.

1. Fast scene reconstruction from sparse view inputs in a single forward pass 2. Real-time rendering of novel views with high quality 3. Efficient per-scene optimization with geometry-aware initialization

MVSGaussian combines Multi-View Stereo (MVS) techniques with 3D Gaussian Splatting to create a hybrid rendering approach that achieves both generalizability and efficiency.

The architecture consists of four main processing stages:

Street Gaussians Neural Splatting (SGNS) enables the representation and rendering of dynamic urban scenes from multi-view camera images and LiDAR data. The system processes data from the Waymo Open Dataset, generates 3D Gaussian-based scene representations, and provides tools for training, evaluation, rendering, and exporting these models.

Key capabilities include: - Processing and integrating data from the Waymo Open Dataset - Creating 3D Gaussian-based representations of static and dynamic scenes - Supporting scene graph modeling for dynamic objects - Providing high-quality novel view synthesis for urban driving scenarios

Emotion-LLaMA addresses the challenge of multimodal emotion recognition and reasoning by integrating multiple data modalities through a unified large language model interface. The system demonstrates several key capabilities:

Multimodal Emotion Recognition: Processes audio, visual, and textual inputs simultaneously using specialized encoders (HuBERT for audio, EVA/MAE/VideoMAE for visual features) that feed into a LLaMA-2-7b backbone.

Emotion Reasoning: Goes beyond classification to provide explanations for emotional states, leveraging instruction tuning to generate human-readable reasoning about detected emotions.

Real-time Inference: Supports multiple deployment interfaces including Gradio demos, REST APIs, and interactive web applications for practical usage scenarios.

Hawk addresses critical limitations in current Video Anomaly Detection (VAD) systems: 1. Superficial semantic understanding of scenes 2. Minimal user interaction capabilities 3. Limited applicability in open-world scenarios due to data scarcity

Unlike traditional anomaly detection systems that simply identify anomalies, Hawk is designed to understand the context and provide detailed explanations about what makes a particular event anomalous, effectively "having eyes like a hawk" in surveillance and monitoring scenarios.

This wiki documents the HDR-GS codebase: a high dynamic range (HDR) novel view synthesis system built on 3D Gaussian Splatting. This page provides a high-level overview of the system architecture, key components, and how they relate to the code structure.

For environment setup and installation instructions, see Environment Setup. For step-by-step training tutorials, see Quick Start Tutorial. For detailed component documentation, see Core Components.

HDR-GS is a novel view synthesis system that generates photorealistic images from arbitrary viewpoints and exposure levels. The system represents 3D scenes using Gaussian primitives and learns to render both Low Dynamic Range (LDR) and High Dynamic Range (HDR) images at 1000x the speed of prior HDR neural rendering methods.

GaussianFormer is an object-centric autonomous driving system that represents 3D scenes using semantic Gaussians instead of traditional occupancy grids. This approach enables efficient and accurate 3D semantic occupancy prediction while significantly reducing memory requirements.

GaussianFormer addresses the challenge of high memory consumption in traditional voxel-based 3D scene representation methods by using an object-centric Gaussian representation. This overview introduces the GaussianFormer system architecture, its core components, and the key differences between the two versions: GaussianFormer and GaussianFormer-2.
LabNex is an open-source Android app designed for managing GitLab projects
[NeurIPS 2024] Diffusion4D: Fast Spatial-temporal Consistent 4D Generation via Video Diffusion Models

GaussianRoom is a research codebase that combines neural implicit surface reconstruction with comprehensive evaluation frameworks for indoor scene modeling. The system takes multi-view RGB-D images as input and produces high-fidelity 3D mesh reconstructions using neural signed distance functions (SDFs) and volume rendering techniques.

The repository is built around the NeuRIS method, which leverages surface normal priors to improve reconstruction quality, particularly for indoor environments with planar surfaces and geometric regularities. It supports multiple data sources including ScanNet datasets and custom captured data processed through Structure-from-Motion pipelines.

MotionLLM is a multimodal system that leverages the capabilities of Large Language Models to analyze and understand human behavior through the joint processing of video content and human motion sequences (SMPL format). Unlike approaches that focus exclusively on either video or motion data, MotionLLM argues that comprehensive human behavior understanding requires analyzing both modalities together to effectively capture body dynamics and semantic meaning.

The framework employs a unified video-motion training strategy that leverages existing coarse video-text data and fine-grained motion-text data to extract rich spatial-temporal insights. This integration enables a deeper understanding of human actions, intentions, and behaviors.

S3Gaussian (Self-Supervised Street Gaussians) is a system designed for autonomous driving scene decomposition using 3D Gaussians without requiring additional supervision such as 3D bounding boxes. The system employs a multi-resolution hexplane-based encoder to encode 4D grids into feature planes and a multi-head Gaussian decoder to generate deformed 4D Gaussians for dynamic scene modeling.

S3Gaussian follows a multi-stage pipeline architecture that processes Waymo dataset through preprocessing, training, and evaluation phases. The system is built around a core Gaussian rendering engine with sophisticated configuration management.

The core training system centers around several key classes and modules that handle the 3D Gaussian representation and rendering:

OpenYOLO3D introduces a novel approach to open-vocabulary 3D instance segmentation that strategically combines existing 2D and 3D models while avoiding computational bottlenecks. The system's key insight is that class-agnostic 3D point cloud instances already contain sufficient geometric information for accurate text-to-3D association without requiring expensive 3D CLIP features.

The system replaces the traditional pipeline of: - Heavy 3D CLIP feature extraction - Multi-view SAM aggregation - Computationally expensive 2D foundation model processing

With an efficient alternative that: - Uses YOLO-World for fast 2D object detection - Leverages Mask3D for class-agnostic 3D instance segmentation - Performs direct text-to-3D mask association

VideoLLaMA2 is a multimodal video understanding system that combines vision encoders, audio encoders (optional), and large language models to process and generate text responses for video content. This documentation provides a comprehensive introduction to the VideoLLaMA2 architecture, its components, and how they work together to enable advanced video understanding capabilities.

VideoLLaMA2 extends the capabilities of the original Video-LLaMA by improving spatial-temporal modeling and adding audio understanding features. The system is designed to perform various tasks including:

- Video question answering (multiple-choice and open-ended) - Video captioning - Audio understanding - Audio-visual reasoning

MLVU addresses the challenge of evaluating how well MLLMs can understand long video content. The benchmark consists of a diverse set of tasks across multiple video types, testing both global and local understanding capabilities. Key features include:

- Long videos ranging from 3 minutes to 2 hours - Varied video genres including movies, tutorials, documentaries, and sports - Multiple task types categorized by their comprehension requirements - Standardized evaluation metrics for fair model comparison

The MLVU benchmark consists of two main datasets and a comprehensive evaluation system:

MLVU tasks are organized into three main categories based on the type of comprehension required:

CF-3DGS (COLMAP-Free 3D Gaussian Splatting) is a system that enables 3D scene reconstruction and novel view synthesis without requiring pre-computed camera poses from external structure-from-motion tools like COLMAP. The system jointly optimizes camera parameters and 3D scene representation using Gaussian splatting techniques, making high-quality 3D reconstruction more accessible for challenging scenarios.

R²-Gaussian (Rectifying Radiative Gaussian Splatting) is a system for fast and direct CT reconstruction using 3D Gaussian Splatting. It implements tomographic reconstruction by representing X-ray attenuation using 3D Gaussians and optimizing their parameters through differentiable rendering.

This system addresses the challenge of reconstructing 3D volumetric data from 2D X-ray projections, commonly known as computed tomography (CT). R²-Gaussian offers:

- Fast reconstruction times compared to traditional methods - High-quality reconstruction results - Support for both synthetic and real-world CT data - Compatibility with both cone beam and parallel beam CT configurations

R²-Gaussian consists of four main components working together to implement tomographic reconstruction:

VLM-Visualizer is a tool for visualizing the attention mechanisms in vision-language models (VLMs), specifically focusing on revealing what parts of an input image a model attends to when generating text responses. This documentation covers the purpose, architecture, and key components of the VLM-Visualizer system. For installation and setup instructions, see Getting Started.

VLM-Visualizer enables researchers and developers to:

1. Visualize which regions of an image a vision-language model focuses on when generating specific tokens in a response 2. Analyze the attention patterns between the vision and language components of multimodal models 3. Generate attention maps by combining the attention weights from both the language model (LLM) and vision transformer (ViT)

This documentation covers the TrimGS repository, a 3D reconstruction system that extends Gaussian Splatting methods with contribution-based trimming for improved geometry representation accuracy. TrimGS provides two main implementations: Trim3DGS (built on 3D Gaussian Splatting) and Trim2DGS (built on 2D Gaussian Splatting), both supporting geometry reconstruction and novel view synthesis across multiple benchmark datasets.

TrimGS implements contribution-based trimming to address geometry inaccuracies in standard Gaussian Splatting approaches. The key innovation involves modifying the differentiable rasterization kernels to identify and remove Gaussians that contribute minimally to the final rendering, resulting in cleaner geometry reconstruction while maintaining rendering quality.

ControlMLLM is a training-free visual prompt learning method for multimodal large language models that enables precise region-based understanding and generation without requiring model retraining. The system optimizes learnable visual prompts at test time using gradient-based attention maps, allowing MLLMs to focus on specific regions of interest indicated by various visual prompt types (boxes, masks, scribbles, points).

The key innovation is that model weights remain frozen throughout the process—only a learnable visual prompt parameter is optimized via gradient descent during inference. This approach:

ShareGPT4Video consists of three primary technical components:

1. ShareGPT4Video-8B: A large video-language model (LVLM) built on the LLaVA framework for video understanding and analysis 2. ShareCaptioner-Video: A specialized model for generating high-quality video captions with multiple inference modes 3. ShareGPT4Video Dataset: A large-scale dataset containing 40K GPT4-Vision-generated video captions and approximately 400K implicit video split captions

The ShareGPT4Video system employs a modular architecture with distinct components that can be used independently or together. The architecture follows a clear processing pipeline from video input to final output.

The ShareCaptioner-Video component supports multiple captioning strategies to balance quality and efficiency:
[ECCV 2024] ShareGPT4V: Improving Large Multi-modal Models with Better Captions

The system integrates several key components: a vision-language model based on LLaVA, a comprehensive data processing pipeline for robotics datasets, an evaluation framework using VIMABench, and object detection capabilities via MaskRCNN. For detailed information about specific subsystems, see Getting Started for setup instructions, Training Pipeline for model training details, and Evaluation Framework for assessment procedures.

LLaRA implements a modular architecture centered around a vision-language policy that processes visual observations and natural language instructions to generate robot actions. The system builds upon the LLaVA framework while adding robotics-specific components for training, evaluation, and deployment.

Mamba-YOLO aims to enhance object detection performance by leveraging the sequence modeling capabilities of Selective State Space Models (SSMs) while maintaining the efficiency and real-time capabilities of the YOLO framework. It provides multiple model variants trained on the MSCOCO2017 dataset for different performance/efficiency trade-offs.

The core innovation of Mamba-YOLO is the integration of State Space Models into the object detection pipeline through several key modules:

LE3D (Lighting Every Darkness with 3DGS) is a research project that enables fast training and real-time rendering for High Dynamic Range (HDR) view synthesis. The system extends 3D Gaussian Splatting to handle noisy RAW images captured in low-light or high-contrast scenes with multiple exposure settings.

Key Capabilities: - Processes RAW DNG images with full demosaicing pipeline - Handles multi-exposure image sets for HDR reconstruction - Supports view-dependent appearance modeling via neural network - Enables real-time HDR rendering in web browsers - Provides adaptive point cloud optimization during training

PGSR (Planar-based Gaussian Splatting Reconstruction) is a system designed for efficient and high-fidelity 3D surface reconstruction from multi-view RGB images. The key distinction of PGSR is its ability to perform reconstruction without requiring any geometric priors such as depth maps or normal maps from pre-trained models. The system leverages Gaussian splatting techniques with planar-based components to achieve accurate surface reconstruction while maintaining computational efficiency.

The PGSR system follows a pipeline architecture that processes multi-view RGB images to produce high-quality 3D surface reconstructions.

The PGSR system consists of several key components that work together to achieve surface reconstruction:

OmniCorpus produces three primary datasets containing 8.6 billion images and 1,696 billion text tokens: `OmniCorpus-CC` (Common Crawl), `OmniCorpus-CW` (Chinese Web), and `OmniCorpus-YT` (YouTube). The system demonstrates production-scale processing capabilities while maintaining strict quality controls and legal compliance.

For immediate usage instructions, see Quick Start. For detailed architectural information, see Architecture Overview. For comprehensive dataset documentation, see OmniCorpus Dataset.

The OmniCorpus system follows a modular pipeline architecture that transforms raw internet data into training-ready multimodal datasets:

The OmniCorpus system consists of five primary processing stages, each implemented as distinct subsystems:

The EVE repository represents a pioneering approach to Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) that fundamentally rethinks the traditional encoder-decoder architecture. Instead of using separate vision encoders to process images before feeding them to language models, EVE employs a pure decoder-only architecture that processes visual information directly within the language model framework.

The EVE project addresses three fundamental research questions that drive the development of encoder-free vision-language models:

Ovis is a multimodal large language model architecture that bridges visual and textual understanding through a novel structural embedding alignment approach. Unlike conventional MLLMs that simply project visual features into the language model's embedding space, Ovis creates a structural alignment between visual and textual embeddings, enabling more effective multimodal reasoning.

The codebase implements several key systems: - Core model architecture with visual tokenization and LLM integration - Training pipeline for instruction tuning - Inference and serving system for model deployment - Support for multiple model variants and sizes

The following diagram illustrates the high-level architecture of the Ovis system:
Code for "Director3D: Real-world Camera Trajectory and 3D Scene Generation from Text" (NeurIPS 2024).

SpatialBot is a multimodal AI framework designed for precise spatial understanding with Vision Language Models (VLMs). This overview provides a high-level introduction to the SpatialBot repository, summarizing its key components, architecture, capabilities, and use cases. For detailed information about specific aspects of SpatialBot, please refer to the corresponding wiki pages listed in the section references.

SpatialBot extends traditional VLMs with enhanced spatial reasoning capabilities, supporting both RGB and RGB-D (RGB + depth) inputs. It is particularly effective for tasks requiring precise spatial understanding, such as object localization, spatial relationship reasoning, and robot manipulation.

TetSphere Splatting is a novel approach for high-quality 3D geometry reconstruction that employs tetrahedral meshes (tet-spheres) as the geometric primitives. The system works by deforming multiple initial tetrahedral spheres to accurately reconstruct 3D shapes through a combination of differentiable rendering and geometric energy optimization. This approach directly yields superior mesh quality without requiring neural networks or post-processing, resulting in significant computational and memory efficiency.

Unlike Eulerian representations (such as DMTet) that use a fixed grid with signed distance values, TetSphere Splatting uses a Lagrangian approach where the geometric primitives themselves move and deform to represent the shape.

VideoLLM-online is an online video large language model designed specifically for processing and interacting with streaming video in real-time. This document provides a comprehensive introduction to the system's purpose, architecture, and key components.

VideoLLM-online is designed to address limitations in existing video understanding models by providing:

1. Real-time streaming video processing (5-15 FPS depending on hardware) 2. Continuous, proactive response updating during video streams 3. Long-form video support (up to 10 minutes) 4. State-of-the-art performance in both online and offline settings

Taming 3DGS is a framework that enhances 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) by providing deterministic primitive count control and implementing performance optimizations to enable high-quality radiance fields on resource-constrained hardware. This wiki page introduces the core concepts, architecture, and key innovations of the Taming 3DGS system.

Taming 3DGS addresses performance limitations in traditional 3D Gaussian Splatting implementations, particularly in resource-constrained environments. The framework introduces:

1. Improved densification control to achieve deterministic primitive counts 2. Low-level optimizations for faster convergence and reduced resource requirements 3. Tools for tracking, visualizing, and benchmarking the optimization process
[CVPR'2025] VoCo-LLaMA: This repo is the official implementation of "VoCo-LLaMA: Towards Vision Compression with Large Language Models".

Aer is a private local storage utility application for Android that functions as a fork of the upstream Anemo project. Unlike traditional file manager applications, Aer integrates deeply with Android's system components to provide seamless, native-feeling access to private storage that remains hidden from other applications.

The key architectural principle of Aer is system integration over standalone functionality. Rather than presenting users with a dedicated file manager interface, Aer hooks into existing Android frameworks including the Storage Access Framework (SAF), Quick Settings, and the system share mechanism.

FlowBot is an Android-based Robotic Process Automation (RPA) system specifically designed for WeChat and WeCom automation. This document provides an overview of the system architecture, core components, and key technologies that enable automated messaging, friend management, and other WeChat/WeCom operations through official Android accessibility services.

FlowBot consists of two primary components: an Android application that performs the actual automation tasks and a task scheduling platform that coordinates execution. The system leverages Android's official accessibility services combined with a self-developed automation framework to interact with WeChat and WeCom applications.

Flash-VStream addresses the challenge of processing long video streams in real-time by introducing a Flash Memory mechanism that performs both temporal and spatial compression of visual features. This enables the model to handle videos of extreme length (hundreds to thousands of seconds) while maintaining real-time processing capabilities and competitive accuracy on video understanding benchmarks.

The system exists in two variant implementations: - Flash-VStream-Qwen: Built on Qwen2-VL-7B-Instruct (ICCV 2025) - Flash-VStream-LLaVA: Built on Vicuna-7b-v1.5 + CLIP-ViT-Large

Both variants share the core Flash Memory mechanism but differ in their foundation models and implementation details.
Long Context Transfer from Language to Vision

MMedAgent is the first multimodal medical AI agent that seamlessly integrates multiple specialized medical AI tools to handle a diverse range of medical tasks across different imaging modalities. The system combines a powerful vision-language foundation model with specialized workers for tasks such as segmentation, detection, classification, and report generation.

MMedAgent uses a microservices architecture with a controller-worker pattern. The system consists of:

1. Central Controller: Coordinates all components and manages communication 2. Specialized Tool Workers: Independent services that perform specific medical AI tasks 3. LLaVA-Med Foundation Model: The core vision-language model specialized for medical applications 4. Web Interface: A Gradio-based UI for user interaction
Download, Install and Share mods easily

Rush is an Android application for discovering, storing, and displaying song lyrics with extensive customization options. The app integrates with Genius and LrcLib APIs to fetch both plain and time-synchronized lyrics, stores them locally in a Room database, and provides rich visualization and sharing features. Built entirely with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose following Material 3 design guidelines, Rush offers both free (FOSS) and commercial (Play Store) distributions with optional in-app purchases.

For architectural patterns and design decisions, see Architecture. For specific feature implementations, see Core Features. For build configuration details, see Build System and Deployment.

Reduced 3DGS extends the original 3D Gaussian Splatting technique with three key memory optimization strategies that collectively achieve a 27x reduction in model size while preserving rendering quality. This document covers the high-level architecture, core components, and optimization techniques implemented in the codebase.

The following diagram illustrates the overall architecture of the Reduced 3DGS system:

Hyper-YOLO is an advanced object detection and instance segmentation framework that integrates hypergraph computation with the YOLO (You Only Look Once) architecture. This page provides a high-level introduction to the Hyper-YOLO system, its key innovations, and overall structure.

Hyper-YOLO addresses limitations in traditional YOLO models by introducing novel components that capture complex high-order correlations among visual features. The system enhances object detection performance through specialized hypergraph-based networks that enable sophisticated feature interactions across different scales and positions.

Hyper-YOLO enhances the conventional YOLO architecture by introducing two major innovations:

TokenPacker is a novel visual projector that adopts a coarse-to-fine compression scheme to generate condensed visual tokens for multimodal LLMs. The system addresses the computational inefficiency of existing multimodal models by significantly reducing the number of visual tokens required for processing images while preserving visual understanding capabilities.

The main TokenPacker algorithm is implemented as a `TokenPacker` class located in multimodal_projector/builder.py:39 and integrates with the LLaVA multimodal architecture to provide efficient visual token processing.

Wild Gaussians addresses the fundamental limitation of traditional 3D Gaussian Splatting when applied to uncontrolled image collections. While standard 3DGS excels with controlled capture conditions, it struggles with:

- Occlusions: Temporary objects (people, vehicles) that appear in some views but not others - Appearance changes: Lighting variations, weather conditions, seasonal differences - Unconstrained capture: Images taken at different times with varying conditions

Wild Gaussians overcomes these challenges by leveraging robust DINO features for consistent scene representation and integrating an appearance modeling module that handles view-dependent appearance variations while maintaining real-time rendering performance.

FadCam is a privacy-focused, open-source Android video recording application designed for discreet background recording with customizable options. KeyboardGPT is an LSPosed Module that integrates generative AI capabilities directly into Android keyboard applications. It intercepts keyboard input to enable seamless interaction with AI language models (like ChatGPT, Gemini, and others) from any application without switching contexts. This page provides an overview of the system, its key features, components, and how they interact.

GS2Mesh is a pipeline for extracting high-quality 3D meshes from Gaussian Splatting representations. The system addresses a fundamental limitation of Gaussian Splatting: while excellent for novel view synthesis, the underlying geometry often contains noise and irregularities. GS2Mesh leverages a pre-trained stereo model as a real-world geometric prior to extract accurate depths from novel views, which are then fused to create a smooth, consistent mesh.

VITA (Vision and Interactive Talking Assistant) is an open-source interactive omni-multimodal Large Language Model (LLM) designed for real-time vision and speech interaction. This document provides a technical overview of the VITA repository, its architecture, core components, and functionality. VITA supports both English and Chinese languages and offers a comprehensive framework for processing images, videos, and audio inputs alongside text, enabling natural multimodal conversations.

The slang-gaussian-rasterization system is a Slang.D implementation of the CUDA-accelerated rasterizer described in the 3D Gaussian Splatting research paper. Rather than providing a complete 3DGS training framework, this repository focuses specifically on the rasterization component and provides API bindings to integrate with existing popular implementations.

The system leverages Slang.D's unified platform capabilities to enable cross-platform rendering while maintaining automatic differentiation support for machine learning workflows. This allows the same rendering codebase to compile for multiple graphics APIs including CUDA, Vulkan, D3D, OpenGL, and OptiX.

Fisheye-GS addresses the fundamental challenge of adapting 3D Gaussian Splatting to fisheye camera models through a modular, extensible architecture. The core innovation lies in the mathematical recalculation of projection transformations specifically designed for fisheye distortion models, while maintaining compatibility with existing 3D rendering pipelines.

The system operates on the principle that only the projection component needs modification to support different camera models, making it highly adaptable and lightweight. Unlike methods that rely on pre-undistortion of fisheye images, Fisheye-GS works directly with distorted fisheye imagery, providing superior visual quality through native geometric handling.

MiniMind-V is a lightweight visual language model (VLM) designed for efficient training and inference with limited computational resources. The system enables language models to understand and process images alongside text, while maintaining minimal computational requirements. MiniMind-V stands out from other VLMs by focusing on extreme efficiency - the smallest variant is only approximately 1/7000 the size of GPT-3, requiring as little as 0.6GB memory during inference.

MiniMind-V builds upon the MiniMind language model, extending it with visual understanding capabilities through the addition of a visual encoder and projection layer. The system follows a standard VLM architecture pattern but is optimized for minimal size and computational requirements.

Florence-VL is a multimodal vision-language model designed to enhance visual understanding through two key architectural innovations:

1. Generative Vision Encoder (GVE): A novel vision encoding mechanism that processes images through a generative approach 2. Depth-Breadth Fusion (DBF): A multi-level feature fusion strategy that combines features from different abstraction levels (detailed local features and global context)

The model is built on the LLaVA codebase and supports two language model backends: - Florence-VL-8B: Uses LLaMA 3.1-8B as the language model - Florence-VL-3B: Uses Phi-3-3B as the language model

Both variants are available as pretrained checkpoints and supervised fine-tuned (SFT) checkpoints on HuggingFace Hub.

Gaussian-LIC addresses the challenge of creating photo-realistic 3D reconstructions in real-time for robotics applications. The system achieves this by:

1. Pose Estimation: Leveraging Coco-LIC (a LiDAR-Inertial-Camera SLAM frontend) to provide robust, accurate 6-DOF pose estimates 2. Scene Representation: Using 3D Gaussian Splatting to represent scenes as collections of 3D Gaussian primitives 3. Real-time Optimization: Employing GPU-accelerated differentiable rendering to optimize Gaussian parameters based on observed camera images

The system operates as a two-component pipeline where Coco-LIC handles sensor fusion and odometry, while Gaussian-LIC focuses on building photo-realistic maps.

SparseVLM is an optimization framework for Vision-Language Models that reduces computational overhead by adaptively sparsifying visual tokens based on textual queries. Unlike previous approaches that use text-agnostic visual sparsification, SparseVLM uses question prompts to guide the selection of relevant visual patches, enabling more efficient inference while maintaining model accuracy.

The key innovation is recognizing that different questions about the same image may require focus on different visual elements. For example, when answering questions about an object in the foreground versus the background, the model should attend to different regions of the image.

SpatialRGPT is a grounded spatial reasoning vision-language model capable of understanding both 2D and 3D spatial arrangements in images. This system processes region proposals (such as bounding boxes or masks) and provides answers to complex spatial reasoning questions about the relationships between objects in visual scenes.

SpatialRGPT is a vision-language model specifically designed for spatial reasoning tasks. Unlike traditional VLMs that may struggle with precise spatial understanding, SpatialRGPT can comprehend complex spatial relationships between objects, including:

- 3D positional relationships (above, below, behind, in front of) - Relative sizes and distances between objects - Containment and support relationships - Complex spatial arrangements involving multiple objects

LiteGS is a refactored implementation of 3DGS that offers improved performance, flexibility, and modularity while preserving the core algorithm of the original implementation.

LiteGS offers several significant improvements over the original 3DGS implementation:

1. Performance: LiteGS achieves a 3.5x speedup compared to the original 3DGS implementation while reducing GPU memory usage by approximately 30%.

2. Modularity: The codebase breaks down the forward and backward passes into multiple PyTorch extension functions, improving accessibility to intermediate variables without modifying C code.

Session is an end-to-end encrypted messenger that minimizes sensitive metadata collection through the use of onion routing via Oxen Service Nodes. The key distinguishing features of Session include:

- Decentralized architecture with no central server - Onion routing for message delivery - End-to-end encryption for all communications - No collection of user metadata - Support for individual messaging, group chats, and communities (open groups) - Local-only storage of messages

DiffGS is a general Gaussian primitive generator based on latent diffusion models, capable of generating Gaussian primitives at arbitrary numbers for high-fidelity 3D rendering. The system disentangles 3D Gaussian Splatting into three specialized functions:

1. GauPF (Gaussian Probability Function): Models the geometry and spatial distribution of Gaussians 2. GauCF (Gaussian Color Function): Handles the color attributes of Gaussians 3. GauTF (Gaussian Transform Function): Controls transformations of Gaussian primitives

This functional decomposition enables efficient modeling and generation of complex 3D scenes represented as Gaussian Splatting primitives.

GaussianAnything is a framework for generating high-quality, editable 3D content from single-view images or text prompts. It was developed by researchers at Nanyang Technological University and accepted at ICLR 2025. The system employs a unique point cloud-structured latent space and a two-stage cascaded diffusion pipeline to produce 3D models represented as surfel Gaussians.

The system's key innovations include: 1. A point cloud-structured latent space that preserves 3D shape information 2. A two-stage generation pipeline for improved quality and control 3. Support for both image-to-3D (I23D) and text-to-3D (T23D) generation 4. Geometry-texture disentanglement for 3D-aware editing

This document introduces the DiffusionGS system, a single-stage image-to-3D generation framework that combines diffusion models with 3D Gaussian Splatting to generate high-quality 3D representations from single images in approximately 6 seconds. This overview covers the system's purpose, architecture, capabilities, and key design decisions.

DiffusionGS addresses the challenge of fast, scalable image-to-3D generation by "baking" Gaussian Splatting into a diffusion denoiser. Unlike multi-stage approaches that rely on 2D multi-view diffusion models or depth estimators, this system performs direct single-stage generation from RGB images to 3D Gaussian representations.

LLaVA-CoT (Chain of Thought) is a multimodal AI system that enhances vision-language models with structured reasoning capabilities. Built on Meta's Llama-3.2-11B-Vision-Instruct model, LLaVA-CoT introduces a four-stage reasoning framework that enables systematic problem-solving for complex visual reasoning tasks.

The system demonstrates superior performance on six challenging multimodal benchmarks compared to larger models like Gemini-1.5-pro, GPT-4o-mini, and Llama-3.2-90B-Vision-Instruct.

The following diagram illustrates the high-level architecture of LLaVA-CoT:

The system consists of four main components:

Xanite is an Android emulator that enables users to play Xbox Original and Xbox 360 games on Android devices. The project integrates open-source emulation cores from the Xemu (Xbox Original) and Xenia (Xbox 360) projects, providing a unified interface for dual-console emulation.

Magma is a foundation model designed specifically for multimodal AI agents. It integrates language processing, image understanding, and action generation into a unified architecture capable of translating goals into actionable plans. The model uniquely bridges perception and action, enabling goal-driven agents to interact with both digital interfaces (UI navigation, gaming) and physical environments (robotics manipulation).

TEXGen is a 3D model generation and texture management system capable of creating detailed 3D models with high-quality textures based on text descriptions. This document provides a high-level introduction to TEXGen's purpose, architecture, and core functionality. For detailed information about specific components, please refer to their respective documentation pages.

1. Text-to-3D Generation: Create complete 3D models from text descriptions 2. Texture Generation: Apply detailed textures to 3D geometry 3. Asset Management: Organize and store generated models using a hash-based system 4. Checkpoint Management: Save and resume generation processes

The TEXGen system processes text descriptions to generate complete 3D models with geometry, materials, and textures.

Speedy-Splat implements a modular architecture centered around an accelerated training and rendering pipeline. The system processes 3D scene data through multiple interconnected components that handle data loading, Gaussian model management, rendering, and evaluation.

The system processes 3D scene reconstruction through a structured pipeline that integrates the two main optimizations seamlessly into the training process.

Speedy-Splat achieves its performance improvements through two primary optimization strategies that address different bottlenecks in the original 3D Gaussian Splatting approach.

The first optimization focuses on precise Gaussian localization within the rendering pipeline, implemented through modifications to the differential renderer submodule.

OpenThinkIMG is an end-to-end open-source framework that empowers Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) to "think with images" by actively using a suite of visual tools to decompose, analyze, and solve complex visual problems. This page provides a high-level introduction to the framework, its key components, and capabilities.

OpenThinkIMG addresses key limitations in current LVLMs by enabling them to interactively use visual tools to analyze and manipulate images. The framework provides:

- Flexible vision tool management and easy integration of new tools - Efficient dynamic inference with distributed tool deployment - A streamlined Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Agent-RL training pipeline, including the novel V-ToolRL method - Standardized evaluation metrics for tool-augmented models

Insight-V is a framework for exploring long-chain visual reasoning with Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). This document provides a high-level introduction to the Insight-V repository, its architecture, key components, and workflows. For detailed information on specific subsystems, please refer to their respective wiki pages.

Insight-V represents an early effort to enhance the visual reasoning capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models. It addresses the challenge of long-chain visual reasoning by integrating three core components:

1. A scalable data generation pipeline that creates high-quality reasoning data 2. A multi-agent system that decomposes visual reasoning tasks 3. A two-stage training pipeline that enhances visual reasoning capabilities

MDPings is a Material Design 3 (MD3) styled Android client built to interact with Nezha monitoring backend APIs. It allows users to:

- Monitor multiple servers simultaneously - View real-time performance metrics - Track historical network status and latency - Connect to server terminals for command execution

The application supports both v0 and v1 versions of the Nezha monitoring API, with adaptive layouts optimized for both phones and tablets.

MDPings follows a clean architecture pattern with distinct layers to maintain separation of concerns and testability.

The following diagram illustrates the key components in MDPings and how they interact:

MDPings offers a comprehensive set of features for server monitoring:

FaithDiff is a framework for faithful image super-resolution using diffusion priors. It aims to enhance low-quality images to high-quality ones while maintaining content fidelity. This page provides an introduction to the FaithDiff system, its components, and how they interact.

FaithDiff addresses challenges in existing image super-resolution approaches by leveraging diffusion models with text conditioning to achieve high-fidelity results, especially with severely degraded images. For detailed usage instructions, see Installation and Setup, for implementation details see System Architecture, and for training information see Training Pipeline.

The Hello Android XR sample repository is designed to:

1. Demonstrate practical implementations of core Android XR features 2. Provide developers with working examples of XR functionality 3. Illustrate best practices for Android XR application development 4. Serve as a starting point for building XR applications

The sample application shows how to incorporate spatial computing concepts into Android applications using official Android XR APIs and libraries.

The sample application demonstrates a typical Android XR application structure, integrating AR/XR capabilities with modern Android development practices.

SplatAD is a CUDA-accelerated rendering library that builds upon the gsplat foundation to provide specialized rendering capabilities for autonomous driving data. The system enables real-time synthesis of both camera images and LiDAR point clouds from 3D Gaussian scene representations, with built-in compensation for motion artifacts and support for the spherical coordinate systems commonly used in LiDAR sensors.

The core system provides two main rendering pipelines: - Camera Rasterization: Traditional perspective projection with rolling shutter compensation - LiDAR Rasterization: Spherical coordinate projection with depth and feature rendering

The following diagram shows SplatAD's architecture with specific code entities and their relationships:

Global Icon Pack is an Xposed module that enables system-wide application of icon packs across Android devices. Unlike traditional launchers that only apply icon theming within the launcher itself, this module extends icon pack support to all system components including SystemUI, Settings app, Recent Apps screen, and other system interfaces.

The Global Icon Pack application is structured as a multi-layered system that operates both as a standard Android application and as an Xposed module runtime component.

The application operates in three distinct modes that determine how icon data is shared between the host application and hooked system processes:

The convex-splatting repository implements "3D Convex Splatting: Radiance Field Rendering with 3D Smooth Convexes" (CVPR 2025), a novel approach to radiance field rendering using 3D smooth convex primitives. This system provides efficient, geometrically meaningful scene representation and synthesis for high-quality novel view synthesis. For detailed installation instructions, see Installation.

The convex-splatting codebase follows a modular pipeline architecture with distinct components for training, rendering, evaluation, and visualization.

Heron is a multiplatform social media application built with Compose Multiplatform. It features a modern, adaptive UI that works across different screen sizes and follows a clean architecture with clear separation of concerns. This overview introduces the main architectural components and systems that make up the Heron codebase.

Heron follows a layered architecture pattern that separates UI, state management, domain logic, and data access:

- scaffold/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/tunjid/heron/scaffold/scaffold/AppState.kt - domain-timeline/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/tunjid/heron/domain/timeline/Timeline.kt - data/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/tunjid/heron/data/repository/TimelineRepository.kt

Delta is an Android application that enhances Wi-Fi hotspot functionality on Stock Android devices (such as Pixel, Motorola, etc.). It provides access to advanced hotspot settings not exposed through the system settings app, enabling users to configure detailed parameters and manage connected devices more effectively. This page provides a high-level introduction to the application's purpose, architecture, and key functionality.

Delta addresses limitations in Stock Android's hotspot management capabilities by exposing hidden settings and providing additional functionality. The app:

Coreply is an Android application that enhances typing experiences by providing intelligent, context-aware text suggestions while users type in messaging applications. The system operates by monitoring user input through Android's accessibility framework, analyzing conversation context, and displaying AI-generated suggestions through overlay interfaces.

Key Characteristics: - Privacy-First: Direct communication with LLM APIs without intermediate servers - Universal Compatibility: Works across 15+ messaging applications - Real-Time Processing: 360ms debounced input processing with caching - Flexible AI Integration: Supports multiple LLM providers and interaction patterns

The following diagram maps Coreply's architecture using actual class and component names from the codebase:

The Olympus repository is a framework for multimodal AI that enables interactions between text and visual content. It provides tools for preparing training data, downloading pre-trained models from Hugging Face Hub, and running inference to generate responses to user prompts. The system is designed to handle a variety of tasks including text generation, image generation and modification, 3D model creation, and video processing.

The Olympus system consists of three main interconnected components:

The data preparation component merges multiple datasets to create comprehensive training data:

This page introduces the Deformable Beta Splatting repository, its purpose, key concepts, and high-level architecture. For implementation details, see Architecture, and for practical usage instructions, see Usage Guide.

Deformable Beta Splatting is a novel rendering technique for representing and visualizing 3D objects that can change form over time. Developed by Liu et al. (2025), this approach extends traditional 3D Gaussian Splatting by using beta functions as the underlying mathematical representation for object shapes.

The key innovation lies in efficiently handling object deformations while maintaining high visual quality, making it particularly valuable for applications in computer graphics, animation, visual effects, and real-time rendering.

NClientV3 is an unofficial Android client for browsing, downloading, and managing NHentai content. This document provides a high-level overview of the application's purpose, architecture, and core features. For information about migrating from the previous version, see Migration from NClientV2.

NClientV3 is a fork of the original NClientV2 project that provides a comprehensive mobile interface for accessing NHentai content. The application is designed for Android devices running Android 8 (API 26) and above, with separate builds optimized for different Android versions. It focuses on providing a secure, customizable, and feature-rich browsing experience with robust content management capabilities.

This page describes the purpose, scope, supported devices, operating modes, and major capabilities of the PlusPlusBattery application. It is the starting point for understanding what the application does and why. For a description of how the code is organized to deliver these capabilities, see Architecture.

PlusPlusBattery is an Android battery information viewer and health estimation tool. It surfaces battery metrics that the standard Android OS does not expose in the system UI, and provides health estimation logic specifically tuned for silicon-carbon anode batteries used in OnePlus, Oppo, and Realme (collectively "OPlus") devices. It also works on general AOSP-based ROMs using a reduced feature set.

Radiance Meshes is a neural radiance field (NeRF) training system that uses tetrahedral meshes as the underlying volumetric representation. The system trains volumetric models from multi-view images by combining adaptive tetrahedral mesh refinement with neural feature networks and differentiable tile-based rasterization.

The repository implements a complete pipeline from raw images to trained volumetric models, organized into five major subsystems:

Liquid is a scalable and unified multimodal large language model (MLLM) framework that seamlessly integrates visual comprehension and generation capabilities within a single model. Unlike conventional approaches that rely on separate models or external pretrained visual embeddings, Liquid employs a unified token space and autoregressive generation paradigm to handle text-to-text, image-to-text, and text-to-image tasks.

The Liquid repository implements a unified approach to multimodal generation, where a single large language model can handle text-to-text, image-to-text, and text-to-image tasks. This is achieved through a shared token space that enables seamless integration of different modalities.

The system consists of several key components:

G-Prune is a graph-based visual token pruning system that addresses computational efficiency challenges in multimodal large language models. The framework uses similarity graphs to model relationships between visual tokens and applies information flow analysis to identify the most representative tokens for retention.

The "Thinking in Space" repository provides utilities for processing and standardizing camera pose data from 3D scene datasets. This codebase extracts positional coordinates from different dataset formats (ARKitScenes, ScanNet, and ScanNet++) and converts them into simplified, consistent formats suitable for downstream computer vision tasks such as 3D reconstruction, novel view synthesis, and spatial analysis.

The repository organizes pose extraction utilities by dataset type, with each script specialized for a particular 3D scene dataset format.

The repository currently supports processing camera pose data from three major 3D scene datasets:

Despite working with different input formats, all scripts follow a common data processing pipeline:

Long-VITA is a multimodal large language model (MLLM) capable of processing long contexts, images, and videos. This document provides a high-level overview of the Long-VITA repository, its architecture, and its key capabilities. For more detailed information about specific components, please refer to their respective wiki pages.

The Long-VITA system is designed to understand and generate responses based on various input modalities: - Text (including very long contexts) - Images (single or multiple) - Videos

It employs a multi-stage fine-tuning approach that progressively builds capabilities from basic vision-language understanding to complex long-form and video comprehension.

uTag is a two-part system consisting of:

1. A modification for Samsung's SmartThings application that bypasses Samsung's device restrictions 2. A companion Android application that provides a user interface and additional functionality

Together, these components allow users to utilize Samsung Galaxy SmartTags on any Android device running Android 11 or above, without requiring root access in most cases. uTag provides nearly all the functionality available in Samsung's official SmartThings app, plus additional features that enhance the SmartTag experience.

uTag operates by applying modifications to Samsung's SmartThings application to remove device restrictions that normally prevent SmartTags from working on non-Samsung devices. These modifications are applied either:

RoboVLMs is a flexible framework for creating Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) for robotics applications. The project aims to transform pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) into systems capable of generating robot actions from visual and language inputs. This overview provides a high-level understanding of the RoboVLMs architecture, components, and workflows.

The RoboVLMs framework consists of four primary components:

1. VLM Backbones: Pre-trained vision-language models that process visual and textual inputs 2. Action Heads: Components that transform VLM outputs into robot actions 3. Training Pipeline: System for fine-tuning VLMs with robotics action data 4. Evaluation System: Frameworks for benchmarking VLAs on standard environments

The ScreenSpot-Pro system consists of four primary subsystems that work together to provide a complete GUI grounding evaluation pipeline:

The system's main components map directly to specific code entities and file structures:

The Mulberry repository is a comprehensive framework for working with Vision-Language Models (VLMs), providing integrated systems for training, inference, and evaluation of various models. This page introduces the key components of the framework and explains how they interact to form a complete VLM ecosystem.

Mulberry serves as a unified environment for:

1. Fine-tuning established vision-language models (Llama, LLaVA, Qwen) with custom datasets 2. Running inference using these models to answer questions about images 3. Evaluating model performance across standard benchmarks 4. Experimenting with advanced reasoning techniques like Compositional Monte Carlo Tree Search (ComCTS)

Mulberry is organized into four primary systems that work together to form a complete VLM pipeline.

Habit-Maker is a reward-based habit tracker for Android that addresses the difficulty of building new habits through:

- Gamification: Streaks (consecutive days completed), points (triangle number formula with streak multipliers), and percentage progress toward the 66-day habit formation threshold - Custom Encouragements: User-defined motivational messages displayed upon habit completion, with success emojis - Contextual Cues: "When/where" fields to help users anchor habits to specific times and locations - Smart Reminders: WorkManager-based notifications with skip-today logic and in-notification actions

Valley Eagle is a cutting-edge multimodal large language model developed by ByteDance that can process and understand text, images, and video data. This document provides a high-level overview of the Valley Eagle system, its architecture, and core components.

The Valley Eagle system is built on the foundation of Qwen2.5 with SigLip vision encoders, incorporating LargeMLP and ConvAdapter components to construct its multimodal projector. In its final version, Valley Eagle references the Eagle architecture, introducing an additional Qwen2VL VisionEncoder that operates in parallel with the original visual tokens to supplement performance in extreme scenarios.

This wiki documents the Parcel Android application, a multi-carrier parcel tracking system that enables users to monitor shipments from 30+ delivery services worldwide. This page provides an overview of the application's purpose, key features, supported carriers, and high-level codebase organization. For detailed technical documentation of specific subsystems, refer to the linked sections throughout this page.

Related Pages: - For detailed architecture patterns and layer interactions, see Architecture Overview - For delivery service integration mechanisms, see Delivery Service Integration - For UI implementation details, see User Interface

WhisperIME is an Android application that provides offline speech recognition capabilities through multiple integration points. It offers a privacy-focused approach by performing all speech recognition locally on the device after an initial model download.

The application serves four primary functions:

1. Standalone Application: A dedicated app for speech transcription and translation 2. Input Method Editor (IME): A keyboard replacement with voice input capabilities 3. System-wide Voice Input Service: An Android RecognitionService implementation 4. Intent-Based Recognition: Support for recognition requests from other apps

WhisperIME offers several key features that differentiate it from other voice input solutions:

This documentation covers CatShare, an Android peer-to-peer file sharing application that implements the 互传联盟 (Interoperability Alliance) protocol for cross-vendor device compatibility. CatShare enables stock Android and international devices to exchange files with Chinese OEM devices (Xiaomi, OPPO, vivo) using their native file sharing systems.

CatShare is a third-party implementation of the 互传联盟 file transfer protocol that allows devices without native support (stock Android, international ROMs, custom ROMs) to participate in the cross-vendor file sharing ecosystem used by major Chinese smartphone manufacturers. The application acts as both a sender and receiver, enabling bidirectional file transfers over Wi-Fi Direct connections.

The objectdetection_script repository is a comprehensive collection of tools, techniques, and improvements for object detection models, with a primary focus on YOLO (You Only Look Once) variants and RT-DETR (Real-Time Detection Transformer). This repository serves as a technical reference and implementation resource for researchers, practitioners, and developers working with object detection models.

This overview document provides a high-level understanding of the repository's structure, key components, and primary systems. For specific implementation details on YOLO improvements, see YOLO Improvement System, for RT-DETR improvements, see RT-DETR Project, and for model optimization techniques, see Model Optimization.

The repository serves as a practical implementation combining state-of-the-art object detection with multi-object tracking capabilities, supporting various input sources including video files, webcam streams, and network cameras. For detailed information about the detection and tracking pipeline, see Core Detection and Tracking Pipeline. For neural network architecture details, see Neural Network Architecture.

The system is built on top of the Ultralytics YOLO framework and integrates several key components to provide end-to-end object detection and tracking functionality:

The repository provides a complete pipeline from training to deployment, with several acceleration paths for optimized inference on different platforms.

YOLOv8 introduces several architectural improvements over previous YOLO versions, focusing on performance, accuracy, and efficiency.

Python scripts performing object detection using the YOLOv8 model in ONNX.

This technical documentation provides an overview of the YOLOv8-DeepSORT-Object-Tracking system, a computer vision pipeline that combines YOLOv8 (a state-of-the-art object detection model) with DeepSORT (Deep Simple Online and Realtime Tracking) to detect and track objects across video frames. The system maintains persistent object identities even when objects move, change appearance, or are temporarily occluded.

This page introduces the core system components, architecture, and data flow at a high level. For detailed information about the internal system structure, see System Architecture. For installation requirements, refer to Installation and Dependencies.

Amethyst is a native Android client for the Nostr protocol, a decentralized social networking protocol. It enables users to create accounts, follow other users, post content, and interact with the Nostr network through relay servers. The application is built using modern Android development practices with Jetpack Compose for the UI layer and Kotlin for all application logic.

The project implements comprehensive support for Nostr Improvement Proposals (NIPs), including text notes, encrypted direct messages, media attachments, Lightning Network payments (zaps), live streaming, communities, wikis, and various other content types. The codebase is designed to be extensible, with a clear separation between protocol logic and platform-specific implementations.

SPIC is an open-source Android application designed to test and demonstrate two Google attestation APIs:

| API | Status | Purpose | |-----|--------|---------| | Play Integrity API | Current | Modern device and app integrity verification | | SafetyNet Attestation API | Deprecated | Legacy integrity verification (still functional) |

The application allows developers and security researchers to perform attestation requests, view detailed verdict information, and understand how these APIs evaluate device and application integrity. SPIC operates in configurable modes, supporting both local testing (on-device) and production-grade server-based verification.

The system performs real-time object segmentation and tracking on video inputs, maintaining consistent object identities across frames while generating visual trails. It extends the Ultralytics YOLOv8 framework by integrating DeepSORT tracking specifically with the segmentation task, creating a complete solution for persistent object monitoring.

Key capabilities include: - Instance Segmentation: Pixel-level object detection using YOLOv8 segmentation models - Multi-Object Tracking: Persistent identity assignment using DeepSORT algorithms - Trail Generation: Visual tracking paths showing object movement over time - Multiple Input Sources: Support for local files, YouTube URLs, and camera streams - Flexible Interfaces: Command-line execution, Jupyter notebooks, and Google Colab integration

WADBS serves as a streamlined interface for managing Android's Wireless ADB debugging feature, which allows developers to connect to their devices over WiFi instead of USB. The application addresses the common developer need to frequently enable and disable wireless debugging while providing additional conveniences like automatic connection data sharing.

PureNGA is an Android modification module that uses the Xposed framework to enhance the user experience of the NGA mobile application (`gov.pianzong.androidnga`). The project implements a sophisticated hook-based architecture to modify various aspects of the target application without requiring direct APK modification.

The system operates through two primary distribution models: - Bundled version: A repackaged NGA APK with modifications integrated directly - Standalone module: An independent Xposed module that hooks into the existing NGA installation

This wiki documents AimYolo, a YOLOv5-based real-time object detection system designed as a targeting aid for shooting games. The documentation covers the complete system architecture, from screen capture through AI-powered detection to input simulation.

AimYolo is an AI-powered targeting assistance system for shooting games that operates as an external overlay application. The system uses the YOLOv5 object detection model to identify targets in real-time game footage and automatically adjusts mouse input to assist with aiming.

YOWOv2 (You Only Watch Once, version 2) is a framework for real-time spatio-temporal action detection in videos. This document provides a high-level introduction to the YOWOv2 system, its architecture, capabilities, and key components. For installation instructions, see Installation and Setup, and for command-line usage details, see Command-Line Interface.

YOWOv2 is designed to detect actions in videos by identifying: - What action is occurring (action classification) - Where the action is occurring (spatial localization via bounding boxes) - When the action is occurring (temporal localization)

It implements a state-of-the-art multimedia recommender system that processes multiple modalities (visual, textual, and audio) to generate personalized recommendations. The system addresses the challenge of effectively integrating heterogeneous multi-modal content while learning robust user and item representations.

FeedFlow is a minimalistic RSS Reader available on Android, iOS, macOS, Windows and Linux. Built with Kotlin Multiplatform, Jetpack Compose and SwiftUI.

The repository includes a complete pipeline for object detection, from model definition and training to inference and evaluation. It supports multiple YOLOv8 model variants with different sizes and performance characteristics.

The YOLOv8-PyTorch implementation consists of four main subsystems: the core model architecture, the training pipeline, the inference pipeline, and utility systems. These components work together to provide a complete object detection solution.

The core of the system is the `YoloBody` class defined in `nets/yolo.py`, which implements the YOLOv8 neural network architecture. YOLOv8 uses a Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) approach for multi-scale feature detection and introduces Distribution Focal Loss (DFL) for improved bounding box prediction.
SocksTun follows a layered architecture with clear separation between the Android application layer, native networking components, and external infrastructure.

The UNICOM and MLCD repository provides a comprehensive framework for building advanced foundational visual models that power multimodal language applications. This page introduces the core technologies, architecture, and key components of the repository, with a focus on how these elements relate to each other within the system.

The repository contains two primary vision foundation models: 1. UNICOM (Universal and Compact Representation Learning for Image Retrieval) - A pioneering approach for creating universal image representations 2. MLCD (Multi-Label Cluster Discrimination) - An evolution of UNICOM that improves semantic understanding

Cirrus is an open-source Android weather application that provides comprehensive weather information through integration with free and commercial APIs. The application offers current weather conditions, multi-day forecasts, hourly predictions, and animated rain radar visualization. It features an extensive widget system for home screen weather displays and supports GPS-based automatic location updates.

Tasky is a highly intuitive and easy-to-use todo list application designed for those who seek a powerful yet minimalistic way to manage their daily tasks.

IdeaMemo is a comprehensive note-taking application designed to quickly capture and organize ideas, thoughts, and memories. It provides users with a clean, intuitive interface and powerful organization features including tagging, calendar views, and location tracking.

IdeaMemo serves as a versatile note management platform with these core capabilities:

1. Rapid capture of text notes and images 2. Organization via tags, dates, and location information 3. Flexible search and browsing functionality 4. Data security through encryption and biometric authentication 5. Comprehensive backup and restoration via local storage and WebDAV

LinkSheet is an Android application that restores Pre-Android 12 link handling behavior by intercepting `VIEW`, `SEND`, and `NFC` intents. The application presents users with a customizable bottom sheet interface to select which application should handle a link, while also applying privacy-focused URL transformations.

LinkSheet intercepts Android link intents and presents an application selection interface before forwarding to the user's chosen application. The core functionality centers around `BottomSheetActivity`, which processes incoming intents through a URL transformation pipeline before displaying available handlers.

ICAFusion is a feature fusion framework for multispectral object detection that leverages dual cross-attention transformers to effectively combine information from RGB (visible) and infrared (IR) images. This system aims to enhance detection performance by capturing complementary information across different modalities, particularly in challenging environmental conditions.

- For installation instructions, see Installation - For in-depth details about the core fusion mechanism, see Dual Multimodal Feature Fusion - For information about data handling, see Data Pipeline

EfficientTeacher is a semi-supervised object detection (SSOD) framework developed by Alibaba Research that extends YOLOv5 to enable training with both labeled and unlabeled data. This framework addresses scenarios where labeled training data is scarce or expensive to obtain, while unlabeled data is abundant. The system implements teacher-student learning paradigms to generate pseudo-labels for unlabeled data and improve model generalization.

EfficientTeacher is built around a modular architecture that separates concerns into distinct subsystems for training, model management, data processing, and configuration.
