GPT Mobile is an Android chat assistant application that enables users to interact with multiple AI language models simultaneously in a single conversation. Unlike traditional chat applications that support only one AI provider, GPT Mobile sends each user message to multiple AI platforms concurrently and displays their responses side-by-side.

The application supports five AI platforms: - OpenAI GPT (GPT-4o, GPT-4, GPT-3.5-turbo) - Anthropic Claude (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3 Opus, Claude 3 Haiku) - Google Gemini (Gemini 1.5 Pro, Gemini 1.5 Flash) - Groq (Llama models, Gemma2) - Ollama (Self-hosted custom models)

All chat history is stored exclusively on-device with no intermediary backend servers, ensuring user privacy. API requests are sent directly to official AI provider APIs.

Capy Reader is an Android RSS reader application that supports multiple backend services through a unified interface. Unlike traditional RSS readers that lock users into a single service, Capy Reader provides flexibility by supporting:

- Local accounts: On-device feed storage with direct HTTP fetching - Feedbin: Cloud-based service at feedbin.com - FreshRSS: Self-hosted RSS aggregator - Miniflux: Minimalist self-hosted reader - Reader API: Any Google Reader-compatible service

The application is built using Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, targeting Android 11+ devices. It is distributed through multiple channels: Google Play Store, F-Droid, IzzyOnDroid, and GitHub Releases.

MeshDiffusion is a diffusion-based generative model that creates 3D meshes by learning to reverse a noise corruption process applied to mesh representations. Unlike traditional mesh generation approaches, MeshDiffusion operates directly on Deep Marching Tetrahedra (DMTet) representations encoded as 3D cubic grids, enabling high-quality mesh synthesis with proper topological structure.

The system supports two primary generation modes: - Unconditional Generation: Creates meshes from pure noise without any conditioning input - Conditional Generation: Completes partial meshes from single-view observations

ThumbKey is a privacy-conscious Android Input Method Editor (IME) designed specifically for thumb-based typing on mobile devices. The application provides a 3x3 grid keyboard layout with swipe gestures for efficient text input, without relying on cloud-based word prediction or keylogging.

It serves as both a research platform for object detection and an educational resource for understanding YOLO architectures.

LVM is a vision pretraining model that converts various kinds of visual data into "visual sentences" and performs next-token prediction autoregressively. It represents a significant advancement in visual modeling by treating visual data as sequences of tokens, similar to how language models process text.

The system is built upon two foundational components: - OpenLLaMA: An autoregressive model that handles sequential token prediction - OpenMuse: A VQGAN (Vector Quantized Generative Adversarial Network) that converts images into visual tokens

LVM is compatible with both GPU and TPU hardware platforms, making it flexible for various computational environments.

- Browse suggested feature flags with known effects - Search and modify any feature flag in Google services - Save frequently used flags and packages - Track Google app updates - Apply multi-flag configurations

The system allows processing of various input sources including camera feeds, video files, image files, and RTSP streams through an intuitive graphical user interface.

The system is built with a multi-threaded architecture to maintain UI responsiveness while performing computationally intensive tasks. It leverages the PyQt5 framework for the user interface and ONNX runtime for efficient AI inference.

SimpMusic is a Free and Open Source Software (FOSS) YouTube Music client built for Android and Desktop platforms using Compose Multiplatform. The application enables users to stream music from YouTube Music and YouTube without advertisements, with support for background playback and offline capabilities.

The project follows clean architecture principles and the MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel) pattern in its UI layer. It leverages Kotlin Multiplatform to achieve approximately 70-80% code sharing between Android and Desktop (JVM) targets.

LLaVA is a multimodal model designed to understand both visual and textual inputs, enabling it to perform tasks such as visual question answering, image-based conversation, and multimodal instruction following. The project aims to create systems with GPT-4-like multimodal capabilities through visual instruction tuning.

mPLUG-Owl is a family of multimodal large language models developed to bridge vision and language understanding. The repository contains three generations of models, each building upon the previous with enhanced capabilities:

1. mPLUG-Owl - The original model focusing on modularization to empower large language models with multimodality 2. mPLUG-Owl2 - An improved model emphasizing modality collaboration between visual and textual information 3. mPLUG-Owl3 - The latest model extending capabilities to long image sequences and video understanding

All models in the family share a common architectural foundation while introducing progressively more advanced features for multimodal understanding and generation.

The mPLUG-Owl family has evolved through several generations:

Twine is a cross-platform RSS reader application built with Kotlin Multiplatform. It provides a modern, feature-rich interface for subscribing to and reading RSS/Atom feeds across Android and iOS devices. This document introduces the application's architecture, key components, and how they interact to deliver the core functionality.

Twine follows a layered architecture pattern with clear separation of concerns:

The architecture follows these principles: - UI Layer: Contains all UI components implemented with Compose Multiplatform - Presentation Layer: Manages UI state and handles user interactions through presenters - Data Layer: Provides data access through repositories that abstract data sources - Platform Integration: Handles platform-specific functionality

The RKNN Multi-Threaded Inference System is a high-performance object detection framework designed to maximize neural processing unit (NPU) utilization on Rockchip RK3588/RK3588s devices. This system achieves significant performance improvements through asynchronous multi-threaded inference, enabling real-time object detection with YOLOv5 models.

This system addresses a common challenge in edge AI applications: maximizing hardware utilization to achieve higher inference throughput. By implementing a thread pool architecture that executes multiple parallel inference operations, the system can increase frame rates by up to 2.6x compared to single-threaded implementations.

It empowers large language models (LLMs) with the ability to comprehend both visual and audio content from videos, enabling natural language conversations about multimodal inputs.

The project extends existing vision-language models by introducing dedicated components for processing temporal information in videos and understanding audio content, resulting in a comprehensive system for video understanding.

This codebase is an unofficial implementation of the paper "3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering" using the Taichi programming language. It provides a complete pipeline for training and rendering 3D scenes represented as collections of oriented 3D Gaussian primitives.

What it does: - Training: Takes multi-view images, sparse point clouds, and camera poses as input, then optimizes a dense point cloud with learned features (rotation, scale, opacity, color) to reconstruct the scene - Inference: Renders novel views from the trained Gaussian representation using a differentiable rasterization pipeline - Visualization: Provides interactive tools for real-time scene exploration and batch rendering

PET-NeuS is a neural surface reconstruction system that extends the NeuS baseline with three key innovations to achieve high-fidelity 3D surface reconstruction from multi-view images. The system represents signed distance fields (SDFs) using a combination of tri-plane representations and multi-layer perceptrons (MLPs), rather than MLPs alone.

The system achieves significant performance improvements over the NeuS baseline: - 57% improvement on Nerf-synthetic dataset (Chamfer distance: 1.97 → 0.84) - 15.5% improvement on DTU dataset (Chamfer distance: 0.84 → 0.71)

Main Entry Point: The system is launched via `run.sh` which executes `exp_runner_pet.py`

1. OCRBench - The original benchmark system with 1,000 question-answer pairs across five OCR components 2. OCRBench_v2 - An expanded benchmark with 10,000 question-answer pairs, bilingual support (English and Chinese), and more diverse OCR tasks

Both benchmarks provide standardized evaluation frameworks to assess how well LMMs can perceive, understand, and reason about text in various visual contexts.

The MultimodalOCR repository is organized into two main directories, each containing its respective benchmark system, datasets, and evaluation scripts.

gptAssist is a lightweight Android application that wraps ChatGPT's web interface within a controlled WebView environment. The application's primary purpose is to provide secure, filtered access to ChatGPT while blocking non-essential URLs and preventing OAuth-based authentication flows.

The application implements a restrictive-by-default approach where only essential domains are allowed, and all other web requests are blocked. This design choice prioritizes security and simplicity over feature completeness.

Primal Android is a native Android client for the Nostr protocol with integrated Lightning Network payment functionality. The application enables users to publish and consume social media content on Nostr while supporting Bitcoin micropayments (Zaps) through Lightning Network integration. It implements a monetization strategy through tiered premium subscriptions (Premium, Pro, and Legend tiers) and offers live streaming capabilities.

The codebase is structured as a multi-flavor Android application supporting two distribution channels: Google Play Store (with proprietary services) and AOSP/F-Droid (pure open-source). Version and build configuration is managed in app/build..

KryptEY is a standalone Android keyboard application that enables secure end-to-end encrypted communication in any messenger application. Built on the foundation of the Simple Keyboard project, KryptEY integrates the Signal Protocol to provide cryptographic security without requiring a centralized server or specific messenger application. The keyboard operates as an Input Method Editor (IME) service within the Android framework while providing encryption capabilities through a separate interface overlay.

The application addresses privacy concerns related to potential legislation that could compromise E2EE in popular messengers by providing an independent, messenger-agnostic encryption solution that operates entirely on-device.

LaVIN (Large Vision-Language Instructed model) is a multimodal AI system that enables efficient vision-language instruction tuning for large language models through a novel Mixture-of-Modality Adaptation (MMA) approach. The system connects image encoders and LLMs via lightweight adapters, achieving superior training efficiency while maintaining strong reasoning capabilities across single-modal and multi-modal instruction-following tasks.

HINT Control serves as a comprehensive management interface for T-Mobile home internet gateways, providing real-time monitoring of cellular signal metrics, WiFi network configuration, and connected device management. The application supports multiple gateway models and offers cross-platform compatibility across mobile and desktop environments.

Fossify Launcher is a privacy-focused Android launcher application that serves as a replacement for the default home screen experience. The application prioritizes user privacy, customization, and performance while maintaining a clean, efficient interface.

Core Identity: - Application ID: `org.fossify.home` (app/build.gradle.kts:30) - Target Platform: Android API 26-34 (gradle/libs.) - License: GNU GPL v3 (LICENSE:1) - Primary Language: Kotlin (app/build.gradle.kts:91)

The launcher registers as a home screen replacement through the Android manifest's `HOME` and `DEFAULT` intent categories (app/src/main/).

Video-ChatGPT is designed to generate meaningful conversations about videos by combining the capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) with a pretrained visual encoder adapted for spatiotemporal video representation. The system can:

- Process and understand video content in detail - Recognize objects, actions, and spatial relationships in videos - Comprehend temporal sequences and event progression - Generate contextually appropriate, detailed responses to video-related questions - Perform creative and generative tasks based on video input - Answer specific questions about video content with high accuracy

The system achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple video understanding benchmarks, including MSVD-QA, MSRVTT-QA, TGIF-QA, and ActivityNet-QA.

VisionLLMv2 is an end-to-end generalist multimodal large language model that significantly expands beyond traditional MLLMs. Unlike most multimodal models that focus primarily on visual question answering with text output, VisionLLMv2 supports a broad spectrum of tasks including object localization, pose estimation, interactive segmentation, and image generation/editing.

The system accepts textual, visual, and in-context instructions to accomplish hundreds of vision-language tasks and demonstrates strong generalization across multiple domains including industry, medical, and remote-sensing images.

VisionLLMv2 follows a modular architecture that integrates vision encoding, language processing, and specialized tools for specific vision tasks.

LLaVA-Med (Large Language and Vision Assistant for Biomedicine) is an AI system that combines vision and language capabilities specifically for the biomedical domain. This page provides a high-level overview of the project, its architecture, and its key components.

LLaVA-Med was developed to bring GPT-4-like multimodal capabilities to biomedical applications, enabling visual instruction understanding and medical image interpretation. The system builds upon the general-domain LLaVA model, specializing it for biomedical content through a curriculum learning approach.

WG Tunnel is an alternative Android client application for WireGuard and AmneziaWG VPN protocols. WG Tunnel was created to address features and support missing from the official wireguard-android app. It combines support for both WireGuard and AmneziaWG protocols with enhanced automation capabilities, making it suitable for users who need dynamic VPN connectivity based on network conditions.

FastSAM (Fast Segment Anything Model) is a CNN-based image segmentation system that achieves performance comparable to the original Segment Anything Model (SAM) at 50× higher run-time speed. The system is built on the YOLOv8 architecture and trained on only 2% of the SA-1B dataset. FastSAM provides multiple prompt modalities—including box, point, text, and "everything" modes—for interactive and automated image segmentation tasks.

MobileSAM is a lightweight implementation of the Segment Anything Model that dramatically reduces model size and inference time while preserving segmentation quality. MobileSAMv2 builds upon MobileSAM by introducing object-aware prompt sampling for even faster "segment everything" capabilities.

MobileSAM achieves remarkable efficiency gains over the original SAM model while maintaining comparable segmentation quality:

| Component | Original SAM | MobileSAM | Reduction | |-----------|--------------|-----------|-----------| | Image Encoder | 611M parameters, 452ms | 5M parameters, 8ms | 99.2% parameters, 98.2% time | | Mask Decoder | 3.876M parameters, 4ms | 3.876M parameters, 4ms | No change | | Total | 615M parameters, 456ms | 9.66M parameters, 12ms | 98.4% parameters, 97.4% time |

MovieChat addresses the fundamental challenge of long video understanding by introducing a sparse memory system that can handle videos with 10K+ frames while maintaining a 10,000× advantage in GPU memory efficiency compared to traditional dense token approaches. The system achieves this through a hierarchical memory architecture that progressively consolidates video information from short-term to long-term memory buffers.

The MRPNN (Multi-Resolution Prediction Neural Network) renderer aims to solve the challenge of rendering complex volumetric effects in real-time by using neural networks to accelerate the rendering process. MU-LLaMA is a music understanding language model that bridges the gap between audio content and textual descriptions. The system processes music audio files and responds to natural language questions about their content, including musical characteristics, instruments, genre, mood, and other descriptive elements.

Core Architecture: MU-LLaMA combines MERT (Music Enhancement with Robust Transformer) audio encoder with Meta's LLaMA (Large Language Model Meta AI) through a custom adapter network. This multimodal fusion enables the system to understand both audio content and textual context simultaneously.

The repository implements the method described in the paper "3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering" and provides tools for training models from multi-view images, visualizing the optimization process, and rendering trained models in real-time.

For details about installation, see Installation and Setup.

The system is built around a central Gaussian Model representation, with dedicated pipelines for training, rendering, and evaluation. The SIBR Viewers provide interactive visualization capabilities, while the Data Handling components manage dataset loading and preparation.

Differentiable Gaussian Rasterization is a technique for rendering 3D Gaussian primitives to 2D images while maintaining differentiability throughout the rendering pipeline. This allows gradients to flow from rendered images back to the 3D Gaussian parameters, enabling optimization of these parameters using gradient descent.

- Differentiability: Supports automatic differentiation for optimization of 3D Gaussian parameters - CUDA Acceleration: High-performance implementation using CUDA for real-time rendering - PyTorch Integration: Seamlessly integrates with PyTorch's autograd system - Spherical Harmonics: Supports view-dependent appearance through spherical harmonics coefficients - Tile-Based Rendering: Efficiently processes image tiles in parallel on the GPU

Amber is a Nostr event signer for Android that implements NIP-55 (Android Signer Application) and NIP-46 (Nostr Connect) protocols. It provides secure private key storage and cryptographic operations (event signing, NIP-04/NIP-44 encryption/decryption) for Nostr applications without exposing private keys to client applications.

The application provides three integration methods: - Android Intents via `nostrsigner://` URI scheme (NIP-55) - ContentProvider API for synchronous operations (`content://com.greenart7c3.nostrsigner.*`) - Nostr Connect (NIP-46) for remote signing via relay events

Amber supports multiple accounts with isolated storage, biometric/PIN authentication, reproducible builds with GPG-signed releases, and optional Tor/Orbot integration for privacy.

For environment setup and execution instructions, see Getting Started. For detailed documentation of individual pipeline stages, see Detection & Tracking Pipeline, Data Interpolation Pipeline, and Visualization Pipeline.

The All-Seeing Project V2 extends vision-language models to comprehend relations between objects in images, not just the objects themselves. The system enables multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) to:

1. Recognize and localize objects within images 2. Understand relationships between objects (e.g., "person riding bicycle") 3. Generate grounded conversations where objects and predicates are linked to spatial regions 4. Parse scene graphs from natural language descriptions

This represents a significant advancement over traditional vision-language models that treat images holistically or focus only on object detection without relationship understanding.

MM-Vet addresses a fundamental limitation in existing vision-language (VL) benchmarks by focusing on integrated capability assessment rather than isolated skill evaluation. Unlike conventional benchmarks that test individual capabilities in isolation, MM-Vet evaluates how well models can combine multiple core VL capabilities simultaneously.

The benchmark evaluates six core capabilities: - Recognition: Object and scene identification - OCR: Text reading and extraction - Knowledge: World knowledge application - Language Generation: Natural language production - Spatial Awareness: Spatial relationship understanding - Mathematical Reasoning: Quantitative problem solving

The differential Gaussian rasterization system serves as the rendering engine for 3D Gaussian Splatting, providing:

- Differentiable rendering: Full forward and backward pass support for neural network training - Real-time performance: CUDA-accelerated tile-based rasterization - Extended outputs: Color, depth, alpha, and radii rendering (modification from original GRAPHDECO implementation) - Flexible input formats: Support for spherical harmonics, precomputed colors, and 3D covariances

The system follows a three-layer architecture where Python provides the user interface, PyTorch extensions handle tensor integration, and CUDA kernels perform the computational work.

Metrolist is a feature-rich Android music streaming application that provides access to YouTube Music content through the unofficial InnerTube API. The application offers advanced playback capabilities, comprehensive library management, multi-language lyrics support with romanization and AI translation, offline downloads, and social features including collaborative listening sessions.

Scope: This page provides a high-level overview of the Metrolist system architecture, core components, and design decisions. For detailed information about specific subsystems, see: - Audio playback implementation: Playback Engine Architecture - YouTube integration details: YouTube Music Integration - Lyrics processing: Lyrics System - External service integrations: External Integrations

The YOLOv8-plate system is designed to automatically detect and recognize Chinese license plates in images. The system provides end-to-end functionality from raw image input to structured text output, including plate location, character recognition, and color classification. The system supports both single-layer and double-layer Chinese license plates across 12 different plate types, from standard civilian plates to specialized government and diplomatic plates.

Dynamic 3D Gaussians extends traditional 3D Gaussian Splatting to dynamic scenes, enabling both high-quality novel-view synthesis and dense 3D 6-DOF tracking. The system represents dynamic objects in a scene as 3D Gaussians that can move and rotate over time, maintaining consistent appearance while capturing complex motions.

The key capabilities include: - Modeling dynamic 3D scenes with moving objects - High-quality novel view synthesis - Dense 3D tracking with 6 degrees of freedom - Persistence of object representations across time - Interactive visualization of learned models

The system architecture of Dynamic 3D Gaussians is designed to be both functional and modular, with most functionality concentrated in a few core Python files.

PointLLM enables large language models to perceive and understand 3D geometric data represented as colored point clouds. Unlike vision-based approaches that rely on 2D projections, PointLLM directly processes 3D point cloud representations (xyz coordinates with RGB color information), avoiding issues with depth ambiguity, occlusion, and viewpoint dependencies.

The system addresses three core challenges: - Modality Gap: Bridging the representation gap between continuous 3D geometric features and discrete language tokens - Data Scarcity: Generating large-scale instruction-following datasets for 3D-language tasks - Evaluation Methodology: Establishing rigorous benchmarks for assessing 3D understanding capabilities

The Qwen-VL repository includes several model variants with increasing capabilities:

| Model | Description | Parameters | Key Features | |-------|-------------|------------|--------------| | Qwen-VL | Base pre-trained model | 7B | Vision-language foundation model | | Qwen-VL-Chat | Aligned conversation model | 7B | Chat interface, multi-round QA | | Qwen-VL-Chat-Int4 | Quantized model | 7B (Int4) | Lower memory, faster inference | | Qwen-VL-Plus | Enhanced visual model | - | Improved detail recognition, high-res images | | Qwen-VL-Max | Most capable model | - | Advanced visual reasoning, optimal performance |

The base models (Qwen-VL and Qwen-VL-Chat) are open-sourced with code and weights, while Plus and Max variants are currently accessible through web interfaces and APIs.

Orgzly Revived is a community-maintained continuation of the original Orgzly Android application. It functions as an outliner, allowing users to take notes and manage to-do lists in a hierarchical structure. The application stores notebooks in plain text using the Org mode format, a powerful markup language originally developed for Emacs.

The app supports synchronization between the local database and various external repositories, including local directories, Git repositories, WebDAV servers, and Dropbox. This allows users to maintain their notes across different devices and platforms.

The addon operates as a self-contained Blender extension that bridges external 3D Gaussian Splatting data with Blender's mesh, material, and geometry node systems.

The system processes 3D Gaussian Splatting data through a multi-stage pipeline that transforms PLY file data into Blender-native representations suitable for interactive editing and rendering.

The addon implements four primary `bpy.types.Operator` classes that handle the complete workflow for Gaussian Splatting data management within Blender.

SAM.cpp is a C/C++ implementation of Meta's Segment Anything Model (SAM), enabling efficient image segmentation directly from C++ applications without requiring Python or PyTorch. This library provides a complete pipeline for performing interactive segmentation using various prompts (points, boxes, etc.), with a focus on efficiency and ease of integration.

The Segment Anything Model (SAM) is a foundation model for image segmentation developed by Meta AI. It's designed to provide high-quality object segmentation masks based on various types of prompts, such as points, boxes, or rough masks. SAM.cpp implements this functionality in pure C/C++, making it accessible for applications where Python dependencies are not feasible.

gsplat is an open-source library designed for high-performance, differentiable rendering of 3D Gaussian primitives. The library serves multiple purposes:

- Research Platform: Provides a foundation for 3D Gaussian Splatting research with state-of-the-art techniques - Production Tool: Enables efficient training and rendering of neural radiance fields using Gaussian representations - Performance Benchmark: Delivers up to 4x memory reduction and 15% speed improvement over reference implementations

The library targets researchers, engineers, and developers working with neural rendering, 3D reconstruction, and novel view synthesis applications.

Canta is an Android application that enables users to uninstall pre-installed (bloatware) and user-installed applications without requiring root access. The application achieves privileged operations by leveraging Shizuku, a service that provides applications with elevated permissions using ADB (Android Debug Bridge) or wireless debugging.

Canta integrates with the Universal Debloater Alliance (UAD) to provide community-curated bloatware classifications and safety recommendations. This integration helps users make informed decisions about which applications are safe to remove.

The system encompasses four major capabilities: (1) YOLOv8-based oriented bounding box detection with specialized model architectures, (2) comprehensive training and validation pipelines with model pruning support, (3) multi-object tracking using both StrongSORT and ByteTracker algorithms, and (4) GPU-accelerated polygon operations for efficient oriented bounding box processing.

The system is built around five core subsystems that work together to provide end-to-end oriented bounding box detection and tracking capabilities.

GrindrPlus is a comprehensive modification framework built using the Xposed framework that intercepts and modifies Grindr's runtime behavior. The system provides two main capabilities:

1. Premium Feature Unlocking - Bypasses subscription restrictions to enable unlimited cascade views, advanced search filters, incognito mode, and other premium features 2. Enhanced Functionality - Adds new features like location spoofing, anti-block detection, unlimited albums, and advanced profile management

The module supports both rooted devices (via LSPosed) and non-rooted devices (via LSPatch), with the latter requiring APK patching and installation through a dedicated manager application.

OTP Helper works by intercepting and analyzing notifications from any application on the device, extracting OTP codes using regex pattern matching, and then performing various actions with those codes according to user preferences. The application is designed to work completely offline without internet permissions, ensuring user privacy by keeping all data on the device.

OTP Helper provides the following key features:

- Automatic detection of OTP codes from all application notifications (not just SMS) - Automatic copying of detected codes to the clipboard - Code history management with privacy features - Notification management for detected codes - Application and notification filtering to ignore specific sources - Extensive configuration options for customizing behavior
[ICLR 2024 Spotlight] DreamLLM: Synergistic Multimodal Comprehension and Creation

The `diff-gaussian-rasterization-w-depth` repository extends the original 3D Gaussian Splatting rasterization engine by adding depth rendering capabilities. This enhancement is primarily intended to support the Dynamic 3D Gaussians project, which requires depth information for advanced rendering effects.

The main features of this repository include: - Forward pass implementation for both color and depth rendering - Support for different depth calculation methods (median and mean) - GPU-accelerated rendering using CUDA - Integration with the Dynamic 3D Gaussians framework

Deformable 3D Gaussians (D-GS) is designed to reconstruct dynamic 3D scenes from monocular video sequences. It extends the 3D Gaussian Splatting approach by incorporating deformation modeling capabilities, allowing for:

- High-quality novel view synthesis - Time interpolation between frames - Real-time rendering of dynamic scenes

Deformable 3D Gaussians combines the efficiency of 3D Gaussian Splatting with a learned deformation field to model dynamic scenes from monocular videos.

Sumire (スミレ) is a privacy-first Android Input Method Editor (IME) for Japanese text input. Built on the principle of absolute privacy, Sumire operates entirely offline with zero network permissions, ensuring that user data never leaves the device. The system provides multiple keyboard layouts, sophisticated Kana-Kanji conversion using dictionary-based graph search, and extensive customization options while maintaining a completely local architecture.

Core Philosophy: - 100% Offline: No `INTERNET` permission declared in `AndroidManifest.xml` - Zero Telemetry: No analytics, cloud sync, or external data transmission - Local Encryption: AES-256 encryption for learning data via `AESEncryptor` - Open Source: MIT licensed with full transparency

This document introduces the LLaVA-RLHF repository, a system for training large multimodal models using Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF). The page covers the Fact-RLHF algorithm innovation, the overall system architecture, and the key components that enable alignment of vision-language models with human preferences while maintaining factual accuracy.

LLaVA-RLHF is the first open-source RLHF-trained large multimodal model designed for general-purpose visual and language understanding. The system builds upon the LLaVA (Large Language and Vision Assistant) architecture and applies reinforcement learning techniques to align the model with human preferences while maintaining factual grounding.

DreamGaussian is designed to create 3D content efficiently, addressing the challenge of generating realistic 3D models from limited input information. The system's core purpose is to convert:

- Single images to 3D models (Image-to-3D) - Text descriptions to 3D models (Text-to-3D) - Multi-view consistent text prompts to 3D models - Combined image and text inputs to 3D models

DreamGaussian employs a two-stage pipeline architecture, with each stage performing specific transformations to generate high-quality 3D content.

DreamGaussian supports multiple input modalities, each configured through specific YAML files that determine the guidance models and processing parameters.

The first stage uses 3D Gaussian Splatting to create an initial representation of the 3D object:

InternLM-XComposer-2.5 (IXC-2.5) is a versatile multimodal large language model that combines vision and language capabilities to perform a wide range of tasks. This document provides a high-level overview of the repository, explaining its architecture, key components, capabilities, and usage patterns.

IXC-2.5 is designed to excel in text-image comprehension and composition tasks, achieving GPT-4V level capabilities with a 7B parameter language model backend. The system supports ultra-high resolution image understanding, fine-grained video analysis, multi-turn multi-image dialogue, webpage generation, and article composition.

- Dynamic novel view synthesis in real-time - High-quality rendering of complex, deforming objects - Temporally coherent scene representation - Efficient optimization and rendering

The 4D Gaussian Splatting system follows a modular architecture designed for efficient training and real-time rendering of dynamic scenes.

4DGaussians is a framework for representing and rendering dynamic 3D scenes with high fidelity and real-time performance. It represents a dynamic scene as a collection of 4D Gaussians (3D space + time) and uses a deformation network to model temporal variations. The system achieves rapid convergence during training (approximately 8 minutes for D-NeRF datasets and 30 minutes for HyperNeRF datasets) and enables real-time rendering of dynamic scenes.

SchildiChat Android Next is a Matrix protocol client for Android, forked from Element X Android. It provides secure, decentralized messaging with end-to-end encryption through the Matrix network. The application is built as a complete rewrite using modern Android development practices, leveraging Jetpack Compose for UI and the Matrix Rust SDK for protocol implementation.

The A-YOLOM system is designed to address the computational efficiency requirements of autonomous driving systems by consolidating multiple perception tasks into a single model while maintaining real-time performance. For detailed information about the core A-YOLOM architecture and training procedures, see A-YOLOM Multi-Task Learning System. For deployment strategies across different platforms, see Deployment and Distribution.

Gaussian Splatting Lightning is a PyTorch Lightning-based framework for 3D Gaussian Splatting, providing a modular, extensible implementation for novel view synthesis and neural scene representation. This page introduces the repository's architecture, core components, and key features.

Gaussian Splatting Lightning adapts the 3D Gaussian Splatting technique into a structured PyTorch Lightning framework to enable:

- Efficient training on single or multiple GPUs/nodes - Multiple renderer implementations and dataset formats - Interactive web-based viewing and scene editing - A variety of cutting-edge extensions to the base algorithm

FERRET is an end-to-end MLLM designed to accept any form of referring expressions and ground anything in its responses. The system enables precise visual understanding and communication by identifying, referring to, and grounding specific objects, regions, or details within images at any level of granularity.

Key contributions of the FERRET project include:

1. A specialized model architecture featuring Hybrid Region Representation and Spatial-aware Visual Sampler components 2. The GRIT dataset (~1.1M samples) for instruction tuning 3. Ferret-Bench, a comprehensive evaluation benchmark for referring, grounding, semantics, knowledge, and reasoning

The FERRET system consists of multiple interconnected components that enable its core capabilities.

Gaussian Splatting is a 3D scene representation technique that models scenes as collections of 3D Gaussian primitives. Each Gaussian is defined by: - Position (xyz): 3D location in space - Rotation (quaternion): Orientation of the Gaussian ellipsoid - Scale: Size along three axes - Opacity: Transparency/alpha value - Spherical Harmonics (SH): View-dependent appearance coefficients

The viewer loads these Gaussians from PLY files and renders them in real-time using either OpenGL shaders or CUDA-based rasterization.

The Gaussian Splatting Viewer follows a modular architecture with clear separation between application logic, rendering backends, and data management:

BakLLaVA is a framework for creating and training multimodal large language models that can process both text and images. It extends various language model architectures (Llama, Mistral, MPT) with vision capabilities, enabling them to understand and generate text based on visual content. This page provides a high-level overview of the system architecture, key components, and workflows.

BakLLaVA combines vision models with language models through a specially designed architecture that projects visual features into the language model's embedding space.

Torch-splatting is a pure PyTorch implementation of 3D Gaussian splatting, a technique for representing 3D scenes using Gaussian primitives. Unlike other implementations that rely on custom CUDA extensions, this repository implements the entire pipeline in PyTorch, making it more accessible and easier to modify.

This wiki documents the COMM (CLIP and DINOv2 Multi-level features Merging) repository, a multi-modal large language model (MLLM) system that integrates dual vision encoders with multi-level feature fusion to achieve state-of-the-art performance on visual-language tasks.

COMM is a PyTorch-based MLLM implementation built upon the LLaVA and Shikra frameworks. The system addresses the vision-language gap in existing MLLMs by integrating complementary visual embeddings from two distinct vision encoders:

- CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training): Provides language-aligned visual features - DINOv2 (Self-DIstillation with NO labels, version 2): Provides self-supervised visual features

The Awesome 3D Gaussian Splatting repository is a curated academic paper database with an automated management system and interactive web interface. It serves as a comprehensive resource for researchers and practitioners working with 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) technology.

Core Functions: - Maintains a structured database of 3DGS research papers in YAML format - Provides automated paper ingestion from arXiv with metadata extraction - Generates a static, interactive web interface for browsing and filtering papers - Enforces data quality through automated validation workflows

The Bluetooth-LE-Spam project is an Android application that leverages the built-in Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) capabilities to create phantom Bluetooth device advertisements. Similar to functionality found in devices like the Flipper Zero, this application can trigger responses on nearby devices, such as popup dialogs or connection requests. The primary goal is to provide a user-friendly interface for BLE advertisement manipulation for educational and research purposes.

This document introduces the core concepts, features, and architecture of the Bluetooth-LE-Spam application. For detailed information about specific subsystems, please refer to their dedicated wiki pages such as the Advertisement System or Spam Detection.

Open Video Editor is an open-source Android application that provides video editing capabilities directly on mobile devices. This document introduces the project's purpose, architecture, and core components at a high level. For more detailed information about specific aspects of the system, please refer to the corresponding sections in this wiki.

Open Video Editor aims to provide a lightweight yet powerful solution for common video editing tasks on Android devices. It prioritizes simplicity, performance, and ease of use while offering essential video editing features. The application is built using modern Android development practices and libraries.

Video-LLaVA is a multimodal AI system that combines visual understanding (for both images and videos) with language processing capabilities. The key innovation is its "alignment before projection" approach, which creates a unified visual representation allowing a single model to effectively process both images and videos without requiring image-video paired data.

Video-LLaVA processes inputs through a multi-stage pipeline that aligns visual features before projecting them into the language model's embedding space:

ShizuTools is a comprehensive Android utility application designed to provide advanced system control capabilities beyond what is typically allowed by the Android operating system. By leveraging the Shizuku privileged access framework, ShizuTools offers a suite of tools that enable users to perform system-level operations without requiring root access.

ShizuTools follows a hub-and-spoke architecture with a central activity that provides access to multiple specialized tool activities. All advanced operations are executed through the Shizuku service, which acts as a privilege escalation bridge.

ShizuTools is built on several key components that work together to provide its functionality:

MPP-Qwen-Next is a multimodal pipeline parallel system that enables efficient distributed training and inference of large vision-language models based on QwenLM. The system combines visual understanding capabilities with conversational AI, supporting image Q&A, multi-turn dialogue, and video conversations through a pipeline parallel architecture optimized for multi-GPU training.

MPP-Qwen-Next is built on the LAVIS framework and implements a multimodal pipeline that processes visual inputs through specialized encoders before feeding them to a large language model. The system supports both distributed training via DeepSpeed and interactive inference through multiple interfaces.

Material Photo Widget is an Android application that allows users to create customizable photo widgets for their home screen. This document introduces the application's purpose, architecture, and core functionality.

Material Photo Widget enables users to "Decorate your home screen with your favorite photos" as its primary function. The application provides:

- Creation of widgets with configurable aspect ratios and shapes - Display of individual photos or cycling through collections - Extensive appearance customization options - Various behavior settings for photo cycling and user interactions

The diff-gaussian-rasterization library enables differentiable rendering of 3D scenes represented as collections of 3D Gaussian primitives. It transforms 3D Gaussians (defined by position, covariance, opacity, and appearance) into 2D images through a tile-based rasterization process. The library supports automatic gradient computation for all input parameters, enabling optimization-based learning of 3D scene representations.

This implementation extends the original 3D Gaussian Splatting rasterizer by adding support for multiple output channels beyond RGB color, including depth maps, normal maps, alpha maps, and custom per-Gaussian attributes. The package is distributed as `diff_gauss` to avoid conflicts with the original implementation.

G-Shell is a generic and differentiable representation for both watertight and non-watertight 3D meshes. The system addresses two key challenges in 3D computer vision:

1. Efficient and robust rasterization-based multiview reconstruction 2. Template-free generation of non-watertight meshes

The project is based on research described in the paper "Ghost on the Shell: An Expressive Representation of General 3D Shapes" (ICLR 2024 oral presentation).

Untracker is an Android application designed to help users remove tracking parameters from URLs before sharing them. Many websites add tracking parameters to URLs to monitor user behavior across the web. Untracker intercepts these URLs and cleans them of tracking information, enhancing user privacy while maintaining the core functionality of the links.

Untracker provides a simple yet powerful way to clean URLs on Android devices. It offers three main features:

1. Untrack when sharing: Users can share links to Untracker via Android's share sheet. Untracker removes tracking parameters and allows users to copy or reshare the cleaned URL.

2. Select to untrack: Users can select text containing URLs, then tap "Untrack" in the selection toolbar to clean and replace the URLs in-place.

LLaVA-Plus (Large Language and Vision Assistants that Plug and Learn to Use Skills) is an extension of the LLaVA vision-language model that learns to use external vision tools to enhance its multimodal reasoning capabilities. The system enables a foundation model to dynamically select and utilize specialized tools for tasks like object detection, image segmentation, and visual recognition.

LLaVA-Plus represents a significant advancement in multimodal AI systems by providing the ability to: - Process and understand both text and images - Identify when specialized vision tools would be helpful - Call external vision tools to obtain additional information - Incorporate tool outputs into its reasoning process - Generate enhanced responses based on combined information

Vectras VM is an Android application that provides full virtualization capabilities on mobile devices, allowing users to run various operating systems including Windows, Linux, macOS, and Android directly on their Android phones and tablets. Based on QEMU (Quick Emulator), this application brings desktop-level virtualization to mobile platforms, enabling users to run multiple virtual machines with different operating systems.

Vectras VM functions as a frontend for QEMU on Android, providing a user-friendly interface for creating, configuring, and running virtual machines. The application manages the entire lifecycle of virtual machines, from ROM selection and disk image creation to VM execution and interaction.

The Monkey project aims to enhance multimodal understanding through several innovations:

1. Improved high-resolution image processing 2. Better text label detection and understanding 3. Document understanding without OCR 4. Efficient processing with multi-scale adaptive techniques

PhysGaussian is a framework that seamlessly combines 3D Gaussian kernels with the Material Point Method (MPM) for physics simulation. Its core innovation is using the same 3D Gaussian primitives for both physical simulation and visual rendering, eliminating the need for intermediate representations like meshes or point clouds.

PhysGaussian consists of three main integrated components that work together to create physically realistic animations of 3D Gaussian scenes.

This component handles the 3D Gaussian representation of a scene, leveraging the original Gaussian Splatting technique. It processes the input 3D scene and prepares Gaussian kernels for physics simulation.

CrossPaste is a cross-platform desktop application that synchronizes clipboard content across multiple devices on a local network using end-to-end encryption and a serverless, local-first architecture. The application monitors clipboard changes on the host device, processes various content types (text, images, files, HTML, RTF, colors, URLs), stores them in a local SQLite database, and synchronizes them to other trusted devices via mDNS device discovery and encrypted HTTP communication.

CrossPaste provides the following key capabilities:

LucidDreamer is a high-fidelity text-to-3D generation framework that creates detailed 3D models from text prompts. This system leverages Interval Score Matching (ISM) and 3D Gaussian Splatting to overcome limitations in existing text-to-3D generation methods.

LucidDreamer addresses a significant limitation in existing text-to-3D generation methods that rely on Score Distillation Sampling (SDS), which often produces inconsistent updating directions for 3D models, resulting in an over-smoothing effect. The framework offers:

- Generation of high-quality 3D models from text descriptions - Efficient processing (~35 minutes on an A100 GPU) - Support for both standard and personalized text-to-3D generation - Higher fidelity textures and shapes compared to SDS-based methods
PG-Video-LLaVA: Pixel Grounding in Large Multimodal Video Models

The C3DGS system is built upon the original 3D Gaussian Splatting framework with significant modifications for compression. The architecture consists of four main subsystems: training orchestration, compression techniques, rendering pipeline, and evaluation framework.

C3DGS implements a multi-stage compression pipeline that targets different aspects of the 3D Gaussian representation. The system achieves over 25x compression compared to standard 3DGS while maintaining rendering quality.

The MMMU Benchmark repository serves as a complete evaluation infrastructure for testing multimodal models' capabilities to understand and reason across multiple academic disciplines. The repository provides:

- Benchmark datasets hosted on HuggingFace (`MMMU/MMMU` and `MMMU/MMMU_Pro`) - Inference scripts supporting four model ecosystems (OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, HuggingFace Transformers, LMDeploy) - Evaluation pipelines for computing accuracy metrics across domain hierarchies - Dataset creation tools for generating vision-only test cases

The system is designed to evaluate models on tasks requiring both visual perception and domain-specific knowledge, ranging from interpreting chemical structures to analyzing architectural diagrams.

GeoChat is a Grounded Large Vision-Language Model (LVLM) specifically designed for Remote Sensing (RS) applications. This page provides a high-level introduction to the GeoChat system, its architecture, capabilities, and purpose.

GeoChat is the first grounded Large Vision-Language Model specifically tailored to Remote Sensing (RS) scenarios. It combines vision and language processing to handle high-resolution satellite and aerial imagery, employing region-level reasoning for comprehensive scene interpretation.

Unlike general-domain vision-language models, GeoChat has been designed with specific consideration for the unique challenges of remote sensing data, including:

Open-LLM-VTuber is a voice-interactive AI companion application that combines real-time speech processing, large language model (LLM) inference, and Live2D avatar rendering. The system enables users to have natural conversations with an AI character that responds with voice, facial expressions, and visual feedback.

Key characteristics: - Multi-modal interaction: Voice input (ASR), text-based reasoning (LLM), voice output (TTS), and visual feedback (Live2D) - Real-time streaming: WebSocket-based bidirectional communication for low-latency interaction - Offline capable: Can run entirely on local hardware without internet connectivity - Cross-platform: Supports Windows, macOS, and Linux with both web and desktop clients - Highly modular: Pluggable ASR, LLM, TTS, and Agent implementations

GS-IR extends traditional 3D Gaussian Splatting to support inverse rendering tasks by decomposing scenes into their constituent material properties (albedo, roughness, metallic properties) and lighting conditions. The system enables:

- Novel view synthesis with high-quality rendering - Material decomposition into physically-based properties - Environment relighting with arbitrary HDRI maps - Shadow and occlusion modeling for realistic lighting effects - Demo generation with dynamic lighting and camera movements

The system operates on standard datasets including TensoIR-Synthetic and Mip-NeRF 360, processing multi-view images with known camera poses to reconstruct editable 3D scene representations.

LightGaussian is a compression framework for 3D Gaussian Splatting (3D-GS) models that achieves up to 15x reduction in model size while maintaining high rendering performance of over 200 frames per second (FPS). The system provides three complementary compression techniques that can be applied sequentially for maximum compression or individually based on specific requirements.

The main objective of LightGaussian is to create compact 3D Gaussian representations through three primary techniques:

1. Pruning: Removing redundant Gaussians 2. SH Distillation: Compressing spherical harmonics coefficients 3. VecTree Quantization: Vector quantization for further size reduction

SuGaR is a method that enables precise and extremely fast mesh extraction from 3D Gaussian Splatting reconstructions. It introduces three key innovations:

1. A regularization term that encourages 3D Gaussians to align well with the surfaces of scenes 2. A method for sampling points on the real surface of scenes using the Gaussian alignment 3. A refinement strategy that binds Gaussians to the surface of a mesh, enabling easy editing, sculpting, rigging, and animation using traditional 3D software

The resulting hybrid representation (mesh + surface-aligned Gaussians) can be rendered with the same quality as original Gaussian Splatting while providing the editability of traditional 3D meshes.

The full SuGaR pipeline consists of four main steps and an optional fifth step:

Mip-Splatting is an alias-free 3D Gaussian Splatting system developed for high-quality novel view synthesis. It extends the original 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) method by introducing advanced anti-aliasing techniques to eliminate rendering artifacts. This page provides a high-level introduction to the system, its key components, and functionality.

Mip-Splatting addresses the aliasing issues in 3D Gaussian Splatting through two key innovations:

1. 3D Smoothing Filter: Adapts the size of 3D Gaussians based on viewing distance to prevent aliasing 2. 2D Mip Filter: Applies appropriate filtering during rendering to handle varying pixel footprints

GaussianEditor is a system for swift and controllable 3D scene editing using Gaussian Splatting representations. This page provides an introduction to the system's purpose, capabilities, and architecture, serving as an entry point to understanding how GaussianEditor works.

GaussianEditor allows users to edit 3D scenes represented as Gaussian splats through an intuitive WebUI. The system enables edits that would otherwise be challenging with traditional 3D editing tools, completing most operations in 2-7 minutes.

- Semantic Tracing: Segment 3D scenes using text prompts or clicks - Edit: Modify selected regions based on text instructions - Delete: Remove objects and automatically inpaint the background - Add: Create and insert new objects through text-guided 2D-to-3D generation

ChartLlama is a multimodal large language model (LLM) specifically designed for chart understanding and generation. This page provides a comprehensive introduction to ChartLlama's purpose, architecture, core capabilities, and usage patterns.

ChartLlama builds upon the LLaVA-1.5 foundation model and uses Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) checkpoints to achieve specialized capabilities in processing and generating chart visualizations. The system is primarily intended for research applications and serves as a demonstration of multimodal AI's ability to interpret and create visual data representations.

The VCD method implements a contrastive probability distribution that reduces over-reliance on statistical bias and unimodal priors. The system modifies the standard text generation process by incorporating both original and noise-distorted visual inputs.

VCD is designed to integrate seamlessly with existing LVLM architectures through a minimal modification approach. The system patches the transformers library's sampling mechanism and provides model-specific integration points.

NaviLLM addresses the challenge of building a generalist agent capable of handling diverse embodied navigation tasks through a unified approach. The system leverages schema-based instruction to cast various tasks into generation problems, enabling a single model to handle:

- Vision-Language Navigation: Room-to-Room (R2R), REVERIE object navigation, Cooperative Vision-and-Dialog Navigation (CVDN), Situated Object Navigation (SOON) - 3D Question Answering: ScanQA for spatial reasoning and question answering in 3D environments - Vision-Language Understanding: LLaVA instruction following for general vision-language tasks

ChatPose is a specialized multi-modal LLM that bridges natural language processing with 3D human pose understanding. It extends the capabilities of vision-language models to not only discuss human poses but also generate them in a standardized 3D representation format (SMPL parameters).

The system can: - Answer questions about human poses in images - Generate 3D human poses from textual descriptions - Reason about pose semantics and biomechanics - Visualize 3D human pose meshes during conversations

ChatPose builds upon the LLaVA (Large Language and Vision Assistant) framework, adding specialized components for 3D human pose understanding and generation. The system integrates visual perception, language understanding, and 3D human body modeling into a unified conversational interface.

This wiki documents the nym-vpn-client repository, a cross-platform VPN client system that provides privacy through the Nym mixnet and WireGuard. The repository contains all client-side components including desktop applications, mobile apps, CLI tools, and the core daemon that manages VPN connections.

NymVPN is a decentralized, privacy-focused VPN system that operates in two modes:

- 5-hop Mixnet Mode: Routes traffic through Nym's mixnet for maximum anonymity, protecting both content and metadata - 2-hop WireGuard Mode: Uses decentralized WireGuard tunneling with AmneziaWG support for censorship resistance

The framework consists of two primary systems: a training pipeline that performs Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) on fine-grained human feedback data, and a comprehensive evaluation system that assesses model performance across multiple benchmarks. This document introduces SceneTex, a neural texture synthesis system designed for generating high-quality, style-consistent textures for indoor 3D scenes using diffusion priors. This overview covers the system's core architecture, processing pipeline, and key components to provide a foundational understanding of how SceneTex operates.

SceneTex is a research system that synthesizes textures for 3D indoor scenes by optimizing a multiresolution texture field using score distillation sampling (SDS) with diffusion priors. Unlike traditional methods that warp 2D views onto mesh surfaces, SceneTex formulates texture synthesis as an optimization problem in RGB space, ensuring style and geometry consistency across multiple viewpoints.

Upscale-A-Video is a framework that transforms low-resolution video content into high-resolution output with enhanced details. The system's key capabilities include:

- Video upscaling with diffusion models - Text-guided enhancement via optional prompts - Temporal consistency across frames - Support for various video formats - Multiple color correction methods

The extension is developed by Ruizhi Shao and Youtian Lin and is installed in threestudio's `custom` directory as a plugin.

The extension provides a complete implementation of Gaussian Splatting training systems, including geometry management, differentiable rendering, optimization strategies, and integration with multiple guidance models for 3D generation tasks.

It provides a unified framework for evaluating LVLMs across 70+ benchmarks with minimal configuration, eliminating the need for manual data preparation across multiple repositories. The toolkit adopts generation-based evaluation for all models and provides results using both exact matching and LLM-based answer extraction methods.

ChatSim is an editable scene simulation system for autonomous driving that enables users to modify virtual driving scenarios through natural language commands. This document provides a high-level introduction to the system's architecture, key components, and how they interact to create realistic scene modifications.

ChatSim allows users to edit autonomous driving scenes by simply describing the changes they want in natural language. The system leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to interpret these instructions and coordinate a series of specialized agents that handle different aspects of the scene modification process. SC-GS is a framework for creating editable dynamic 3D scenes through the use of sparse control points that drive 3D Gaussians. This system enables both high-fidelity novel view synthesis and intuitive motion editing of dynamic scenes from monocular video input.

SC-GS represents dynamic scenes by using a set of sparse control points that drive the deformation of 3D Gaussians. This approach provides two key capabilities:

1. High-fidelity dynamic view synthesis - Rendering novel viewpoints of dynamic scenes with high visual quality 2. Interactive motion editing - Enabling users to intuitively manipulate the motion of objects in the scene

LLaVA-Grounding is a multimodal AI system that enables natural language conversations about images with precise visual grounding. Unlike traditional visual chat systems that only generate text responses, LLaVA-Grounding can:

- Identify and segment specific objects or regions mentioned in conversations - Ground textual descriptions to precise pixel-level masks and bounding boxes - Respond to visual prompts (clicks, boxes, masks) with natural language descriptions - Perform part-whole reasoning about objects - Execute multi-turn conversational interactions with visual context

The system integrates three foundational technologies: the LLaVA vision-language model, the OpenSeeD unified vision framework, and the Semantic-SAM visual prompting system.

The Deblurring 3D Gaussian Splatting system consists of several interconnected components that work together to reconstruct sharp 3D scenes from blurred input images.

The system addresses the core challenge of view synthesis in sparse data scenarios, making it particularly valuable for applications where capturing dense viewpoints is impractical or expensive. Unlike traditional NeRF-based approaches that require hundreds of views, FSGS achieves comparable quality with significantly fewer input images while maintaining real-time rendering performance.

The FSGS system consists of several interconnected components that work together to enable few-shot view synthesis:

SmartEdit integrates multiple state-of-the-art models into a unified framework capable of processing complex image editing instructions. The system builds upon Vicuna LLM, LLaVA vision-language model, and InstructDiffusion to create a powerful multimodal editing system.

SmartEdit employs a sophisticated two-stage training approach that progressively builds the model's capabilities from basic vision-language alignment to complex instruction-based editing.

The SmartEdit system integrates multiple model components through a sophisticated token-based communication system and parameter-efficient fine-tuning approach.

Omni-VideoAssistant is a video-language AI model system built on the foundation of the LLaVA (Large Language and Vision Assistant) project with enhanced capabilities for video understanding. The system provides a unified interface for querying both static images and video content using natural language, delivering contextual responses based on visual analysis.

The system is distributed as the `omni_video_assistant_6_1` model via Hugging Face and offers multiple interaction methods including web-based and command-line interfaces. It represents an evolution from earlier versions (5.3 → 6.1) with improved performance and capabilities.

The Omni-VideoAssistant system follows a modular architecture centered around the core model with multiple interface layers:
Intrinsic Image Diffusion for Single-view Material Estimation

DiffusionLight estimates lighting conditions from a single image by "painting" a chrome ball into the scene using Stable Diffusion models. This approach bypasses the limitations of traditional methods that require extensive HDR panorama datasets. Instead, DiffusionLight leverages diffusion models trained on billions of standard images to generate physically-plausible mirror balls that reflect the scene's lighting.

The system produces HDR environment maps in OpenEXR format that can be used for: - Photorealistic object insertion into existing scenes - Relighting 3D objects to match scene illumination - Image-based lighting (IBL) in rendering pipelines - Scene understanding and analysis

Grendel-GS addresses the scaling limitations of single-GPU 3D Gaussian Splatting implementations by distributing training workloads across multiple GPUs. This allows for:

- Significantly faster training: Up to 3.5x speedup with 4 GPUs - Support for larger models: Handling 40+ million Gaussians compared to the ~11 million limit of single-GPU implementations - Higher-resolution scene reconstruction: Direct training on 4K resolution scenes - Compatible replacement: Maintains algorithmic compatibility with the original 3DGS implementation

The following diagram illustrates the high-level architecture of Grendel-GS:

The training pipeline orchestrates the entire training process, from initialization to model saving. It manages data loading, distributed workload division, and optimization.

ColorBlendr is an Android application designed to customize Material You color schemes on devices running Android 12 and above. It offers enhanced control over the color theming system introduced with Android's Material You (also known as Monet), allowing users to fine-tune accent colors, background saturation, lightness, and apply custom color schemes to individual applications.

The purpose of ColorBlendr is to extend Android's Material You theming capabilities beyond the default options provided by the system. By utilizing either root access or Shizuku, ColorBlendr can modify system-level color resources to create personalized color schemes that apply across the entire system or to specific applications.
Official implementation of project Honeybee (CVPR 2024)

The system implements two distinct generation pipelines (NeRF and DMTet) and supports both single-prompt generation and batch processing workflows. For specific implementation details of individual components, see Core Concepts, Generation Pipelines, and Advanced Features.

RichDreamer's architecture centers on a novel Normal-Depth diffusion approach that generates multi-view consistent 3D geometry and appearance. The system replaces traditional RGB-based diffusion guidance with specialized Normal-Depth models trained on the G-buffer Objaverse dataset.

LangSplat is a 3D Language Gaussian Splatting system that combines natural language features with 3D scene representation through Gaussian Splatting techniques. It enables the creation of 3D scenes that incorporate language-based semantic information, allowing for novel applications in scene understanding, object localization, and semantic segmentation in 3D space.

LangSplat extends the capabilities of traditional 3D Gaussian Splatting by incorporating language features directly into the 3D representation. This integration enables:

- Semantic querying of 3D scenes using natural language - Language-driven 3D object localization - 3D semantic segmentation with language features - Novel view synthesis with semantic understanding

Pixelix is a Kotlin Multiplatform client application for Pixelfed, the federated image-sharing social network. While originally developed as an Android application, Pixelix has evolved into a full multiplatform solution supporting Android, iOS, and desktop (JVM) platforms through shared business logic and UI components.

The application enables users to browse, post, and interact with content on the Pixelfed network, providing features such as direct messaging, customizable theming, widgets, and comprehensive social media functionality including timelines, profile management, and content creation tools.

Chat-Scene is a state-of-the-art multi-modal large language model that integrates 3D point cloud data with 2D multi-view images to achieve superior performance in 3D scene understanding tasks. The system excels in three primary capabilities:

- 3D Grounding: Locating objects in 3D scenes based on natural language descriptions - Scene Captioning: Generating descriptive text for 3D scenes and objects - Question Answering: Answering questions about 3D scene content and spatial relationships

The system achieved #1 rankings on both ScanRefer and Scan2Cap benchmarks as of September 2024, demonstrating its effectiveness in real-world 3D scene understanding applications.

MGIE (MLLM-Guided Image Editing) is an implementation of instruction-based image editing that leverages Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to enhance the editing process. Developed as part of the research paper "Guiding Instruction-based Image Editing via Multimodal Large Language Models" (ICLR'24 Spotlight), MGIE addresses the challenge of brief or ambiguous human instructions by using MLLMs to generate more expressive and detailed instructions for image editing.

MoE-LLaVA (Mixture of Experts for Large Vision-Language Models) is a framework that enhances Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) by applying the Mixture of Experts (MoE) approach. The main innovation is using sparse expert activation where only a subset of experts process each token, achieving high performance with fewer activated parameters. This document provides an overview of the system architecture, key components, and how these elements interact.

MoE-LLaVA enhances multimodal language models by incorporating Mixture of Experts layers. The model processing pipeline works as follows:

1. Input Processing: - Images are processed through an image processor to create visual embeddings - Text is tokenized using the appropriate tokenizer for the base model

Splatter Image is a deep learning system that reconstructs 3D objects from a single 2D input image. The system uses a neural network to predict 3D Gaussian parameters (position, rotation, scale, opacity, and color) that represent the 3D structure of an object. These parameters are then rendered using a differentiable Gaussian Splatting renderer to produce novel views of the object.

Key features: - Ultra-fast single-view 3D reconstruction - Ability to reconstruct any object (trained on large-scale datasets) - End-to-end differentiable pipeline - Support for multiple datasets (ShapeNet, CO3D, Objaverse, Google Scanned Objects) - Pre-trained models available via Hugging Face - Interactive demo for uploading and reconstructing user images

GauStudio is a modular research framework that supports and accelerates development in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) and its diverse applications. The framework provides a unified interface for working with heterogeneous 3D reconstruction datasets, configurable model architectures, and multiple rendering backends.

Scope: This document provides a high-level overview of GauStudio's architecture, core components, and design principles. For specific implementation details, refer to the specialized pages: Dataset System, Camera System, Configuration System, Models & Renderers, and Tools & Workflows.

[CVPR 2024] VCoder: Versatile Vision Encoders for Multimodal Large Language Models

DG-Mesh is a framework that reconstructs high-fidelity, time-consistent 3D meshes from monocular videos. Given monocular inputs of dynamic scenes and camera parameters, this system reconstructs high-quality surfaces with appearance information and tracks mesh vertex motion across time frames. DG-Mesh handles topology changes effectively and can reconstruct challenging structures like thin surfaces (e.g., bird wings).

This overview introduces the core components and functionality of the DG-Mesh system. For detailed information about specific components or processes, please refer to the respective wiki pages referenced throughout this document.

SUPIR (Scaling Up to Excellence for Photo-Realistic Image Restoration) is a cutting-edge system designed for high-quality photo-realistic image restoration and upscaling in the wild. This document provides a high-level overview of SUPIR's capabilities, architecture, and usage patterns.

SUPIR addresses the challenge of restoring and enhancing degraded images to achieve photo-realistic results. It can:

- Restore low-quality, compressed, or degraded images - Upscale images to higher resolutions (configurable scaling factor) - Generate photo-realistic enhancements while maintaining fidelity to the original content - Leverage natural language guidance through LLaVA integration for contextual restoration

Self-Organizing Gaussians is a 3D scene compression system that extends the original 3D Gaussian Splatting method with a novel sorting algorithm called PLAS. The system represents 3D scenes as collections of millions of 3D Gaussians, then organizes these Gaussians into 2D attribute grids that can be efficiently compressed using standard image codecs.

Key Innovation: The PLAS sorting algorithm transforms unstructured 3D Gaussians into spatially coherent 2D grids, enabling compression ratios of 10x (174MB → 17MB) while maintaining visual quality.

The C3DGS system consists of several interconnected components that work together to compress, render, and evaluate 3D Gaussian splats.

The C3DGS system follows a pipeline architecture where the original 3D Gaussian splats are processed through a compression pipeline, then visualized through a rendering pipeline, and evaluated through quality metrics.

The compression pipeline is the central component of the C3DGS system, responsible for reducing the size of 3D Gaussian splat representations.

OpenTune is a feature-rich Android application that provides an enhanced YouTube Music experience without advertisements, offering background playback, offline downloads, synchronized lyrics, and advanced audio controls. The application is built using modern Android development practices with Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, and follows Material Design 3 guidelines.

OpenTune is distributed as an Android application with the package identifier `com.Arturo254.opentune` for production releases and `com.Arturo254.opentune.debug` for debug builds. The current version is 2.0.0 (build 114) targeting Android API level 35 with minimum support for Android 7.0 (API 24).

GaussianAvatars is a system for creating photorealistic 3D head avatars using a combination of 3D Gaussian splatting techniques and the FLAME parametric head model. It enables the creation of high-fidelity, animated head models from multi-view images that can be rendered in real-time.

GaussianAvatars addresses the challenge of creating realistic, animatable 3D head avatars by combining: - The structural advantages of the FLAME parametric head model - The rendering quality of 3D Gaussian splatting - A binding mechanism that connects the two representations

CAPod (Companion App for AirPods) is an Android application that enables AirPods and Beats headphones to work seamlessly with Android devices by providing features typically reserved for Apple devices. The application monitors Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) advertisements broadcast by Apple audio devices and decodes Apple's proprietary Continuity Protocol to extract device status information.

The application operates without requiring root access or system modifications. It leverages Android's standard Bluetooth APIs and BLE scanning capabilities to achieve compatibility. CAPod is open-source under GPL v3 and available through multiple distribution channels including Google Play, F-Droid, and GitHub Releases.

The application utilizes encrypted preference storage to securely maintain duress password configurations and supports both password-based and length-based detection methods across different Android keyguard implementations.

The YOLOv5 Fire Detection system is designed to:

The architecture follows the typical object detection pipeline: input data is processed by the YOLOv5 model, which performs feature extraction and object detection specifically trained to identify fires. The results are then visualized with appropriate metrics.

YOLO3D is an unofficial implementation of the paper "3D Bounding Box Estimation Using Deep Learning and Geometry" by Mousavian et al. The system detects objects in 3D space from 2D images, primarily for autonomous driving applications. Unlike the original paper which used Faster-RCNN for 2D detection and VGG19 for regression, this implementation uses YOLOv5 for 2D detection and ResNet18/VGG11 for 3D parameter regression.

USB HID Client is an Android application that transforms a rooted Android device into a USB Human Interface Device (HID), enabling it to function as a keyboard and mouse for any connected computer. The application operates at the Linux kernel level by creating and managing character devices (`/dev/hidg0` and `/dev/hidg1`) through the USB gadget framework. Since HID communication occurs at the hardware protocol level, no software installation is required on the target computer, and the functionality extends even to pre-boot environments such as BIOS/UEFI firmware interfaces.
